<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093496 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00012: (2026x) Assert constraint

- page_id: `298093496`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093496/2026x+Assert+constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:10:27.842+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements > (2026x) Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An assert constraint usage specifies that the result of a given constraint must always be true. If, at any point, the assert constraint evaluates to anything other than its asserted value, it indicates a logical inconsistency within the model.

There are two ways a constraint can be asserted:

50%

- by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the assert keyword (unnamed element) or the assert constraint keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']

50%

- by containing an owned assert constraint usage, i.e., the asserted constraint is the assert constraint usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the assert constraint keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='Assert constraint_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assert constraint' space='UAF2P']

##### Asserting a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model

###### Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor

To assert constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of an element for which you want to reference a constraint, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed assert constraint, declare the assert keyword, then follow with the name of the constraint you want to reference.
  2. For a named assert constraint, declare the assert constraint keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the constraint you want to reference. To negate an assert constraint usage, specify the *not* keyword after the *assert*keyword, e.g., *assert not constraint*.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

constraintdefMassLimit{ 
attributemass:MassValue; 
attributemassLimit:MassValue; 
mass<=massLimit 
} 
assertconstraintmassIsPositive{ 
attributemass:MassValue; 
mass>0[kilogram] 
} 
 
partvehicle{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue; 
assertmassIsPositive{ :>>mass=massRequired; }// step 1.a - unnamed usage reference subsets another constraint usageassertconstraint positiveMass::>massIsPositive{ :>>mass=massRequired; }// step 1.b - named usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
assertnotmassIsPositive{ :>>mass=massRequired; }// negated assert usage}

###### Asserting constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To assert constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an assert constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on an element symbol executing the Create Assert Constraint command.

###### Asserting constraints via the Specification panel

To assert constraints via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the Asserted Constraint field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the assert constraint usage. The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.

##### Asserting a constraint via the owned assert constraint usage itself as the asserted constraint

###### Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor1Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor

To assert constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword assert constraint for an assert constraint usage. To negate an assert constraint usage, specify the *not* keyword after the *assert*keyword, e.g., *assert not constraint*.
2. Specify the element name .
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle{ 
attribute massActual : MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

assertconstraintmassIsPositive{ massActual<=massRequired }// named assert constraint usageassertconstraint{ massActual<=massRequired }// unnamed assert constraint usage 
assert notconstraintmassIsPositive{ massActual<=massRequired }// named and negated assert constraint usage 
}

###### Asserting constraints via the Create Element command

To assert constraints via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click assert constraint to create an assert constraint usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).

###### Asserting constraints via the view palette1Asserting constraints via the view palette

To assert constraints via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click the **assert constraint** button to create an assert constraint usage element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the constraint.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).

###### Asserting constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To assert constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the assert constraint element.
  2. Select an element symbol and on the right side of the assert constraints compartment, click the Create assert constraint button.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The new element is created and displayed in the assert constraints compartment.

###### Asserting constraints via the Specification panel2Asserting constraints via the Specification panel

To assert constraints via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **assert constraint**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **assert constraints**compartment section's bar, click the **Create assert constraint**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The element is created in the assert constraints compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>An assert constraint usage specifies that the result of a given constraint must always be true. If, at any point, the assert constraint evaluates to anything other than its asserted value, it indicates a logical inconsistency within the model.</p><p>There are two ways a constraint can be asserted:</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddda2df6-d4a6-454b-8e84-cef579d35c5f"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fbe2b56f-d9a4-4dd7-b8dd-270b8b8fd8d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="77cc1c2f-73ee-45f7-8401-d9fea30fd095">by <strong>referencing </strong>a constraint defined elsewhere in the model. <ul><li>The element symbol is displayed with the <em>assert </em>keyword (unnamed element) or the <em>assert constraint</em><span> keyword (named element).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Assert constraint_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li data-uuid="a4fd0e5c-63ff-4a78-bdfe-b496dde8a9b0"><span>See the procedures:<br /></span><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link>:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d843be2-d2d8-4955-9f57-b77850271cee"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="ccf46370-a1dc-49af-a78a-9cde35af4b25">by <strong>containing </strong>an owned assert constraint usage, i.e., the asserted constraint is the assert constraint usage itself.<ul><li>The element symbol is displayed with the <em>assert constraint</em> keyword.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Assert constraint_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>See the procedures: <ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting a constraint via the owned assert constraint usage itself as the asserted constraint"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link>:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the Create Element command"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the view palette1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Asserting constraints via the view palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the symbol compartment in a view"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Asserting constraints via the Specification panel2"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Asserting constraints via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Asserting a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model</h3><h4>Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To assert constraints via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e48287a0-b228-4138-9e22-f068eea98135">In the body of an element for which you want to reference a constraint, declare one of the following:<ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For an unnamed assert constraint, </span>declare the <em>assert </em><span>keyword, then follow with the name of the constraint you want to reference.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For a named assert constraint, </span>declare the <em>assert constraint </em><span>keyword, name the new usage, and then <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the constraint you want to reference.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="706294d1-23b2-4cf8-ab0b-b0104fb07349"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">To negate an assert constraint usage, specify the <em>not</em> keyword after the <em>assert </em>keyword, e.g., <em>assert not constraint</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="a238e6ff-691b-4020-b23b-256524e5d986">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.</li><li data-uuid="65cbc619-5f9a-47e6-8637-db19a778d1b3">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2129a857-5450-433c-8e6e-f87b64c160bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span unnamed usage reference subsets another constraint usage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"> style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">assert</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">not</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Asserting constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To assert constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="fc116859-95b6-4f95-b44a-ea889038796c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an assert constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on an <span>element symbol executing </span></span>the <strong>Create Assert Constraint</strong> command.</li></ul><h4>Asserting constraints via the Specification panel</h4><p>To assert constraints via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="29cb9c24-fa85-46d4-81f3-3f1399aee5d7">Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the <strong>Asserted Constraint</strong><strong> </strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the assert constraint usage. <span><br /></span>The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3>Asserting a constraint via the owned assert constraint usage itself as the asserted constraint</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3eb2fbfe-39f0-4f7c-b6f7-6d53fdb6102a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Asserting constraints via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To assert constraints via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="64f1c1ab-95d9-4c72-8450-c0e9259401ef">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword <em>assert constraint</em><span> for an assert constraint usage.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2721b72c-a7a5-487f-95fb-fd2626958c40"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">To negate an assert constraint usage, specify the <em>not</em> keyword after the <em>assert </em>keyword, e.g., <em>assert not constraint</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="445694ea-64c3-492f-94c6-eab24662d094"><span>Specify the element name</span>.</li><li data-uuid="59eda3ea-f6a1-401a-ac7a-363c3b1d2d39">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.</li><li data-uuid="38abd981-5807-4279-844f-ac1e8621e177">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9b64aea-929b-4975-bfca-dc683c372c98"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MassValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    assert</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> massIsPositive </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">{ massActual<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &lt;= </span>massRequired }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">           </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named assert constraint usage<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    assert</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">{ massActual<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &lt;= </span>massRequired }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                                    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed assert constraint usage<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    assert not</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> massIsPositive<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">{ massActual<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &lt;= </span>massRequired }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>// named and negated assert constraint usage</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Asserting constraints via the Create Element command</h4><p>To assert constraints via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="15ae49f2-38f7-4286-b9b6-553ec725c854"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="8db4a7f3-cc86-4358-96b5-65ee46543e47">In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, click <strong>assert constraint </strong>to create an assert constraint usage element.</li><li data-uuid="9ac686c3-c691-474e-bd0e-c5baeeaaaea6">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="84f81f3a-81ec-4a9d-889d-a4fba370b91f">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c06b2c2-61d7-4294-b17e-7343c39f7f70"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Asserting constraints via the view palette1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Asserting constraints via the view palette</h4><p>To assert constraints via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1ca56548-a640-450f-a028-caf8178e2030">In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click the<span> <strong>assert constraint </strong></span>button to create an assert constraint usage element.</li><li data-uuid="d98af073-3fcd-4de1-a1b2-18ac5092298d">Click in the view pane where you want to create the constraint.</li><li data-uuid="26698172-842b-43e1-8a72-5a49d7974968">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).</li></ol><h4>Asserting constraints via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>To assert constraints via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6a5c4559-7d57-4f80-b06d-afb2b5825735">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select an element symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>assert constraint </strong>element.</li><li>Select an element symbol and on the right side of the <strong>assert constraints </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create assert constraint </strong>button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ec4d7a0c-ad3a-4475-9167-3d2e8196c034">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).<br />The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>assert constraints </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22a51950-38b1-45f8-9f45-5da62ba76f32"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Asserting constraints via the Specification panel2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Asserting constraints via the Specification panel</h4><p>To assert constraints via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5270dc1f-3d54-49d0-9ca0-13c8fbb5db1c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1606836c-9e01-4b0c-a215-350140c9f8e5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>assert constraint </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>assert constraints </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create assert constraint </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Assert constraint" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="86b4300a-a22a-4b3d-9481-1a77e6990862">Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).<br />The element is created in the <strong>assert constraints </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093837 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00013: (2026x) Assign action

- page_id: `298093837`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093837/2026x+Assign+action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:22:09.878+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An assignment action is used to change a *referent* feature of a *target*occurrence to a new *assigned value*. The target is specified as the result of an expression and the referent is specified as a feature chain relative to that target. The new value for the feature is determined as the result of a different expression. When the assignment action usage completes, the referent feature has the new assigned value for the target occurrence.

- The target must be an occurrence, because the values of the features of attributes do not change over time.
- If the referent feature has a multiplicity upper bound other than 1, then an assignment action can assign multiple values to it, consistent with the multiplicity of the feature. The values are all assigned atomically, at the same time.

##### Specifying assign actions

###### Specifying assign actions via the Textual Editor

To specify accept actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the assign action and do one of the following:
  1. For a named assign action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword assign .
  2. For an unnamed assign action usage, declare the keyword assign .
2. Follow with an expression that evaluates to the target and a feature chain identifying the referent, separated by a dot, followed by the symbol := and an expression whose result is the assigned value.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

actiondefRecordNames{ 
initemrecord:Record; 
initementries:Entry[1..*];

// The values of *entries.name* are assigned to the *names* feature 
// The target of the assign action usage is *record*, and *catalog.names* is the feature chain 
assignrecord.catalog.names:=entries.name;

// The value of the expression *entries.name + entries.surname* is assigned to the *record* feature 
// The target of the assign action usage is implicitly the action def *RecordNames* 
assignrecord:=entries.name+entries.surname; 
}

###### Specifying assign actions via the view palette

To specify assign actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the assign button to create an assign action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the assign symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the element via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The new value for the feature is determined as the result of a different expression. When the assignment action usage completes, the referent feature has the new assigned value for the target occurrence.</p><ul><li>The target must be an occurrence, because the values of the features of attributes do not change over time.</li><li>If the referent feature has a multiplicity upper bound other than 1, then an assignment action can assign multiple values to it, consistent with the multiplicity of the feature. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093604 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00014: (2026x) Assume constraint

- page_id: `298093604`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093604/2026x+Assume+constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:16:57.508+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements > (2026x) Requirement constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An assume constraint states the assumed constraints imposed on the model, expressed formally or informally in the requirement.

There are two ways these constraints can be assumed:

50%

- by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the assume keyword (unnamed element) or the assume constraint keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P']

50%

- by containing an owned assume constraint usage, i.e., the assumed constraint is the assume constraint usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the assume constraint keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='assume constraint 1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Assume constraint' space='UAF2P']

##### Assuming a constraint by referencing a constraint defined elsewhere in the model

###### Assuming constraints via the Textual Editor

To assume constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of a requirement for which you want to reference a constraint, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed assume constraint, declare the assume keyword, then follow with the name of the constraint you want to reference.
  2. For a named assume constraint, declare the assume constraint keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the constraint you want to reference.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

constraintmassIsPositive{attributemass:MassValue;mass>0[kilogram]}

requirementdefMaximumMass{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

assumemassIsPositive{// step 1.a - unnamed usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
:>>mass=massRequired; 
}

assumeconstraintpositiveMass::>massIsPositive{// step 1.b - named usage reference subsets another constraint usage 
:>>mass=massRequired; 
} 
}

###### Assuming constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To assume constraints via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] on a requirement element symbol executing the Create Assume Constraint command.

###### Assuming constraints via the Specification panel

To assume constraints via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a constraint element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the **Assumed Constraint** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the assume constraint element. The appropriate constraint is specified for the element.

##### Assuming a constraint via the owned assume constraint usage itself as the assumed constraint

###### Assuming constraints via the Textual Editor1Assuming constraints via the Textual Editor

To assume constraints via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword assume constraint for an assume constraint usage
2. Specify the element name.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementdefMaximumMass{ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

assumeconstraintpositiveMass{// named constraint 
massRequired>0[kilogram] 
} 
}

###### Assuming constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To assume constraints via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the assume constraint element.
  2. Select an element symbol and on the right side of the assume constraints compartment, click the Create assume constraint button.
2. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The new element is created and displayed in the assume constraints compartment.

###### Assuming constraints via the Specification panel1Assuming constraints via the Specification panel

To assume constraints via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **assume constraint**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **assume constraints**compartment section's bar, click the **Create assume constraint**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.). The element is created in the assume constraints compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093739 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00015: (2026x) Attribute

- page_id: `298093739`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093739/2026x+Attribute
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:34.623+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An attribute definition is a definition of attributive data that can be used to describe systems or parts. An attribute definition defines a set of data values, such as numbers, quantitative values with units, qualitative values such as text strings, or data structures of such values. An attribute usage is a usage of an attribute definition. An attribute usage is always referential, whether or not the *ref*keyword is used explicitly in its declaration. The data values of an attribute usage are constrained to be in the range specified by its definition. The values of an attribute usage are data values that remain constant over time unless the attribute is owned by an occurrence, such as an item, part, or action, in which case the value can change over the occurrence's lifetime to reflect its changing condition.

##### Creating attribute usage and definition elements

###### Creating attribute usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create attribute usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *attribute def*for an attribute definition.
  2. *attribute* for an attribute usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the attribute's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

attributedefSensorRecord;// attribute definition

attributetemperature:'°C';// attribute usage

###### Creating attribute usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create attribute usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. attribute def to create an attribute definition element.
  2. attribute to create an attribute usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the attribute's features.

###### Creating attribute usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create attribute usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:
  1. The **attribute** def button to create an attribute definition element.
  2. The **attribute** button to create an attribute usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **attribute**or the **attribute def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the attribute symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the attribute's features.

###### Creating attribute usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create attribute usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an attribute and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click attribute .
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an attribute and on the right side of the attributes compartment, click the Create attribute button. The attribute is created and displayed in the attributes compartment.

###### Creating attribute usage elements via the Specification panel

To create attribute usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] of the element for which you want to create an attribute.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click **attribute**in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **attributes**compartment's section bar, click the **Create attribute**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The attribute is created in the **attributes** compartment.

###### Auto-creating attribute definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create attribute definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093289 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00016: (2026x) Auxiliary symbolic view symbols

- page_id: `298093289`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093289/2026x+Auxiliary+symbolic+view+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:58:36.279+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View kinds > (2026x) Graphical views > (2026x) Symbolic views > (2026x) Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Auxiliary symbolic view symbols encompass symbols available inthe **Common**group of the symbolic view palette.

##### Text box

A text box allows you to type any text, either plain or HTML text.

You can format the symbol styles in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'].

###### Creating a text box

To create a text box

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click one of the following:
  1. The **text box** button to create a text box for plain text.
  2. The **text box (html)** button to create a text box for HTML text. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the**text box******or the**text box (html)******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the symbol and type the needed text.

###### Converting between plain text and HTML text in the text box

To convert between plain text and HTML text in the text box

- In the view pane, click the text box, then the button PLAIN to convert to plain text or HTML for HTML text.

##### Separators

You can use a horizontal separator to rule off different parts of a view. The**Rotate**button allows changing the separator from horizontal to vertical position, and vice versa.

You can format the symbol styles in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P']. The symbol text can be displayed as plain text or HTML text.

###### Creating a separator

To create a separator

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click one of the following:
  1. The **vertical separator** button.
  2. The **horizontal separator** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **vertical separator**,**horizontal separator**, or**rectangular shape**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the symbol.

###### Changing the separator's position

To change the separator's position

- Select the separator on the symbolic view's pane and click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Rotate button at the corner of the selection.

##### Rectangular shape

You can use a rectangular shape to delineate different parts of a view.

You can format the symbol styles in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P']. The symbol text can be displayed as plain text or HTML text.

###### Creating a rectangular shape

To create a rectangular shape

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click the rectangular shape button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **vertical separator**or**horizontal separator**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the symbol.

##### Image from file

The image from file command allows you to insert an image as a symbol in the view pane.

###### Inserting an image

To insert an image

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click the **image from file** button.
2. Click in the view pane. The Open dialog opens.
3. Select the needed image and click Open .

Alternatively, you can also insert an image as a symbol in the view pane:

- by dragging and dropping an image from the file system directly onto the view pane.
- by copying an image from the file system and pasting it into the view pane.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093159 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00017: (2026x) Basic textual notation tasks

- page_id: `298093159`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093159/2026x+Basic+textual+notation+tasks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:35:14.831+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x) Managing textual notation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Review the procedures on this page for basic textual notation tasks.

###### Moving selected textual notation

You can move selected portions of textual notation with a simple drag and drop. This functionality is more efficient than copying, deleting, and pasting the data you want to move.

To move selected textual notation

1. Select the portion of the textual notation you want to move to a different part of the notation. You can do that with:
  1. Left mouse button - select consecutive notation.
  2. Mouse's scroll wheel - select notation in a rectangular shape (see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Basic textual notation tasks' space='UAF2P'] procedure).
2. Click the left mouse button on the selected portion.
3. Drag the selection to the intended location.

###### Common basic textual notation procedures

- For procedures on creating, deleting, copying, pasting, or cutting the textual notation using the Textual Editor, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Copy/paste notation using rectangular selection

You can select the textual notation in a rectangular shape by using your mouse's scroll wheel. This functionality is especially useful when you need to copy large portions of aligned notation or copy/paste repetitive data.

To copy/paste notation using rectangular selection

1. 
  1. Click and hold your mouse's scroll wheel, then select the portion of the notation you want to copy.
  2. Click Ctrl+C shortcut keys to copy the selection. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  3. Click and hold your mouse's scroll wheel, then select the portion of the notation you want to replace with the notation copied in Step 1.
  4. Click Ctrl+V shortcut keys to paste the copied notation. The selected notation is replaced with the copied one. [ATTACHMENT filename='rectangular_selection_2.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Review the procedures on this page for basic textual notation tasks.</p><h4>Moving selected textual notation</h4><p>You can move selected portions of textual notation with a simple drag and drop. This functionality is more efficient than copying, deleting, and pasting the data you want to move. </p><p>To move selected textual notation</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the portion of the textual notation you want to move to a different part of the notation. You can do that with:<ol><li>Left mouse button - select consecutive notation.</li><li>Mouse's scroll wheel - select notation in a rectangular shape (see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Copy/paste notation using rectangular selection"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic textual notation tasks" /></ac:link> procedure).</li></ol></li><li>Click the left mouse button on the selected portion.</li><li>Drag the selection to the intended location.</li></ol><h4>Common basic textual notation procedures</h4><ul><li>For procedures on creating, deleting, copying, pasting, or cutting the textual notation using the Textual Editor, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Copy/paste notation using rectangular selection</h4><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">You can select the textual notation in a rectangular shape by using your mouse's scroll wheel. This functionality is especially useful when you need to copy large portions of aligned notation or copy/paste repetitive data. </p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;">To copy/paste notation using rectangular selection</p><hr style="margin-left: 40.0px;" /><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ol><li>Click and hold your mouse's scroll wheel, then select the portion of the notation you want to copy.</li><li>Click Ctrl+C shortcut keys to copy the selection.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectangular_selection_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic textual notation tasks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>Click and hold your mouse's scroll wheel, then select the portion of the notation you want to replace with the notation copied in Step 1.</li><li>Click Ctrl+V shortcut keys to paste the copied notation.<br />The selected notation is replaced with the copied one.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectangular_selection_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic textual notation tasks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093514 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00019: (2026x) Binding

- page_id: `298093514`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093514/2026x+Binding
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:16:22.607+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Connectors
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A binding is a binary connection that:

- asserts that the connected features have equal values in the same context. See the procedures on this page.
- binds a referential feature in one context to a composite feature in another context to assert they are the same thing. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_binding.png']

###### The image displays the *fuelFlowBinding* binding connector where *fuelTank.fuelFlowOut* is bound to *engine.fuelFlowIn.*Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

##### Binding elements

###### Binding elements via the Textual Editor

To bind elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the *binding*keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), the keyword bind, then the name of the first element you want to bind, followed by the textual symbol =, then the name of the second element you want to bind. If the binding has no name and no specialization, the keyword *bind*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button. partdefVehicle{ 
partfuelTank:FuelTank{ 
outfuelFlowOut:Fuel; 
} 
partengine:Engine{ 
infuelFlowIn:Fuel; 
} 
bindingfuelFlowBindingbindfuelTank.fuelFlowOut=engine.fuelFlowIn;// named binding 
bindfuelTank.fuelFlowOut=engine.fuelFlowIn;// unnamed binding 
}

###### Binding elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To bind elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the bind command.
2. Click on the element you want to bind. A binding path is created between the elements.

###### Binding elements via the view palette

To bind elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **bind** button to create a binding usage.
2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to bind, then on the second. A bind symbol is created between the elements.

###### Binding elements via the Create Relationship command

To bind elements via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to bind.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click binding . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to bind. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to bind and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to bind from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

See the procedures on this page.</li><li>binds a referential feature in one context to a composite feature in another context to assert they are the same thing.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_binding.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094176 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00020: (2026x) Calculation

- page_id: `298094176`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094176/2026x+Calculation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:40:05.307+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Analysis & Verification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A calculation is a kind of action that has a distinguished parameter with direction *out*called the result parameter. A calculation specifies a reusable computation that returns a result in the result parameter. In addition to its parameters, a calculation may have features that are calculation or action usages that carry out steps in the computation of the result of the calculation. The calculation may also have other features that are used to record intermediate results in the computation. A calculation always has a result parameter, inherited if not owned, which can be declared as an out parameter using the keyword *return* instead of *out*.

For information on how to evaluate calculations, see [Invoking calculations](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=invoking-calculations&version=2026x) in SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin documentation.

##### Creating calculation usage and definition elements

###### Creating calculation usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create calculation usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *calc def*for a calculation definition.
  2. *calc* for a calculation usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. Specify the calculation's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. calcdefDimensions{ 
inlength:Real; 
inwidth:Real; 
return:Real; 
} 
calcarea:Dimensions{ 
// Parameters redefine parameters of Dimensions 
inlength; 
inwidth; 
returnresult; 
length*width 
//Result expression is implicitly bound to the return parameter 
}

###### Creating calculation usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create calculation usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. calc def to create a calculation definition element.
  2. calc to create a calculation usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the calculation's features.

###### Auto-creating calculation definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create calculation definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

The calculation may also have other features that are used to record intermediate results in the computation. ## PAGE 00021: (2026x) Case

- page_id: `298094148`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094148/2026x+Case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:39:02.634+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Analysis & Verification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A case is a kind of calculation that produces a result intended to achieve a specific objective regarding a given subject. A case is a general concept that may be used in its own right, but also provides the basis for more specific kinds of cases, including analysis cases, verification cases, and use cases.

##### Creating case usage and definition elements

###### Creating case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *case def*for a case definition.
  2. *case* for a case usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for cases' space='UAF2P'] in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. casedefFaultRecovery{// case definition 
subjectsystem:AutomationSystem; 
actorengineer:Person; 
objective{ 
doc 
/* The engineer determines the cause of the systemfault and resolves it returning the system tonominal operation.*/ 
} 
}casefaultRecovery:FaultRecovery;// case usage

###### Creating case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. case def to create a case definition element.
  2. case to create a case usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for cases' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Auto-creating case definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create case definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092831 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00022: (2026x) CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition

- page_id: `298092831`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092831/2026x+CATIA+Magic+Cameo+SysML+v2+Community+Edition
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-25T09:50:02.793+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

With the Object Management Group (OMG) releasing **SysML v2**, the community is eager to explore and learn this new language. To support this journey, our teams are introducing **CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition**– a game-changer for anyone looking to master**SysML v2**!

- 100% Standard Conformance
  - Full adherence to the OMG SysML v2 specification metamodel.
- Textual and Graphical Syntax in Perfect Sync
  - Instant two-way synchronization between textual and graphical representations.
- Designed for Learning and Training
  - Model up to 500 major elements - ideal for practice and language exploration.
- Free of Charge

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Downloading the CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition

To download the CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition

1. Go to https://software.3ds.com/
2. Create an account (if you don't have one yet) and sign in.
3. Click Free Download > CATIA Magic/No Magic .
4. Find CATIA Magic SysML v2 Community Edition in the list.
5. Click 1/5 for Windows, 2/5 for Mac, 3/5 for Linux, 4/5 for Windows no install, or 5/5 for Mac aarch. [ATTACHMENT filename='download_community_ed.png']
6. Install the product you just downloaded (Magic Systems of Systems Architect, or MSOSA for short).
7. Launch the Magic Systems of Systems Architect.
8. In the License Manager, select Use in Community Mode . [ATTACHMENT filename='License Manager.png']
9. Read the license agreement and click I agree .
10. Once the Welcome screen loads, you will see a prompt indicating that the SysML v2 plugin must be installed.
11. Click the Install button in the prompt. The Resource Manager opens with the SysML v2 plugin already selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resource Manager.png']
12. In the Resource Manager , click the Download/Install button to install the SysML v2 plugin.
13. Restart the tool. You are now running the modeling tool in SysML v2 Community Edition Mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092805 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00023: (2026x) CATIA SysML v2 Solution

- page_id: `298092805`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092805/2026x+CATIA+SysML+v2+Solution
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-02-03T10:45:58.104+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### THE MOST POWERFUL, INTEGRATED, AND 100% STANDARD-COMPLIANT SYSML V2 SOLUTION

We invite you to watch the video from our September 2025 webinar showcasing our SysML v2 solution overview.

400249

##### CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition: For Learning and Teaching - Completely Free

With the Object Management Group (OMG) releasing **SysML v2**, the community is eager to explore and learn this new language. To support this journey, our teams are introducing **CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition** – a game-changer for anyone looking to master **SysML v2**!

- 100% Standard Conformance
  - Full adherence to the OMG SysML v2 specification metamodel.
- Textual and Graphical Syntax in Perfect Sync
  - Instant two-way synchronization between textual and graphical representations.
- Designed for Learning and Training
  - Model up to 500 major elements - ideal for practice and language exploration.
- Free of Charge

For download instructions, click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) CATIA Magic/Cameo SysML v2 Community Edition' space='UAF2P'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### SysML v2 Quick Sheet

For your convenience, the Dassault Systèmes and OOSE co-authored SysML v2 quick sheet is available for download below as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language using our SysML v2 solution.

###### [ATTACHMENT filename='2025-10 SysMLv2 Cheatsheet.pdf'][IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Click the image, then click Download in the top-right corner to download the full file.

##### Standard Conformance

Full compliance with the SysML v2 standard is at the core of our implementation.

- Built on principle – our team makes standard compliance a core value; every engineer is committed to it.
- Interoperability – models exchange seamlessly with other tools following the standard.
- Ecosystem-ready – full conformance simplifies integration across the partner ecosystem.
- Future-proofing – no risk of vendor lock-in or compatibility gaps as the standard evolves.
- Trust & Transparency – engineers know their models are pure SysML v2, not tool-specific variants.

By delivering 100% standard conformance, Dassault Systèmes ensures that customers benefit from the**full power of SysML v2.**

##### Text and Diagrams in Perfect Sync

CATIA Magic/Cameo products deliver**seamless two-way synchronization**between**textual**and**graphical**SysML v2 model representations.

- Instant consistency – changes in text or diagrams are immediately reflected in both views.
- Dual approach – textual syntax appeals to software developers, while diagrams provide clarity for complex system architectures.
- Unique capability – currently the only solution supporting 100% of the SysML v2 standard with true two-way synchronization.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Dynamic Views

SysML v2 supports**three model visualization options**:

- Graphical
- Tabular
- Textual

In CATIA Magic/Cameo, users can**dynamically generate graphical and tabular views**by specifying**expressions that query the model**.

- Automatic updates – views can be rebuilt automatically when the model changes, saving systems engineers significant time and effort.
- Reusable definitions – view definitions can be stored in libraries and reused across projects and teams.
- Efficient modeling – eliminates manual adjustments.

Dynamic views are one of the most powerful capabilities in CATIA Magic/Cameo, enabling**efficient, consistent, and reusable visualizations**throughout the organization.

400249

400249 400249

##### Model Evaluation

SysML v2 introduces a powerful expression language for concise navigation and analysis of models. Using dot‑notation for feature chaining and collection operators such as sum(), complex queries can be written in a compact, intuitive form. Expressions are evaluated in full model context, honoring inheritance, redefinitions, subsets, and library functions.

CATIA Magic/Cameo enhances this with features that make analysis seamless:

- Requirements verification – any requirement violations are immediately flagged, providing instant traceability to violated constraints.
- Roll-ups & trade studies – apply roll-up patterns, perform “what-if” analyses, and conduct trade-off studies directly on the model.
- Evaluation in Model or Console – run expressions directly within the model or use the Evaluation Console to execute custom expressions without modifying the model.
- Advanced evaluation – supports analysis at both model and meta-model levels.
- Interactive diagrams – adjust initial values to test system behavior without modifying the model.

This combination of language and product capabilities**bridges modeling and analysis**, enabling**data-driven decision-making**and**full traceability from design exploration to validation**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

400249

##### Adopt SysML v2 at Your Own Pace

Organizations can adopt SysML v2 at their own pace thanks tothe**full co-existence of SysML v2 with SysML v1**. Both versions can be authored in the same CATIA Magic/Cameo modeling environment, with the ability to select SysML v1 or v2 per project. Migration is supported by the SysML v2 transformation specification, ensuring legacy models remain usable and protecting existing investments.

Key features:

- Authoring – co-existence of SysML v1 and v2 in the same environment
  - Choose v1 or v2 on a per-project basis
  - Support for SysML v2 transformation specifications for smooth migration, including diagrams
- API – server REST API continuity
  - Full SysML v2 standard API support
  - Continue using existing server REST API for both v1 and v2 projects
- Simulation – use case continuity from v1
  - Core SysML v1 simulation use cases supported in SysML v2
- User experience – focus on usability
  - Best features from SysML v1 implementation reused to maximize user efficiency

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

400249****

##### Standard SysML v2 REST API: CI/CD workflows, automation, querying, model evaluation

Teamwork Cloud fully conforms to the**SysML v2 API and Services**specification, ensuring interoperability with external tools and deployment of automated MBSE workflows.

- Programmatic access – create, edit, query, retrieve, and evaluate models directly from scripts or applications.
- Seamless integration – connect with data analytics platforms, DevOps CI/CD pipelines, and custom engineering workflows.
- Custom API extensions – query and analyze SysML v2 model more efficiently with CATIA Magic's home-grown Model Evaluation services. Stay tuned for more exciting API extensions!

This combination enables engineers to effectively**navigate, query, and analyze SysML v2 models**with full standard compliance and interoperability in mind.

##### Collaborative Modeling Through Teamwork Cloud

**Teamwork Cloud (TWC)** enables secure, lock-free collaborative modeling for SysML v2.

- Simultaneous collaboration – multiple users can work on the same model at the same time.
- Automatic merging – non-conflicting changes are merged automatically; intuitive resolution for overlaps.
- Versioned & modular models – decomposition into manageable units, supporting efficient collaboration across teams, organizations, and geographies. Supports collaborative development in branches for experimentation, with reintegration through a model patch mechanism.
- Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) – fine-grained permissions at the project or branch level to protect data access, ensuring that users and user groups have appropriate access rights.
- Support for U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) requirements
  - Provides support for Data Markings/Classification based on the DoD Manual 5200.01 Volume 2 specification, allowing to mark security-sensitive data such as users and projects with custom-defined data markings
  - Data spillage handling via dedicated tools for physical removal of model data affected by data spillage incidents
  - On-premise deployment for isolated classified programs
  - Prime-sub model exchange workflows built with scalability and IP protection in mind
- Standards-based SAML authentication integrations – supports single sign-on including built-in, LDAP, PKI/CAC, and SAML-based authentication.
- Auditing – provides access to audit reports to track user access and permissions granted for server projects, ensuring compliance and traceability.
- Cross-cluster synchronization – enables model updates to be shared securely across multiple server clusters, facilitating distributed collaboration.

Integration with 3DEXPERIENCE enables large-scale projects and supports multi-discipline integration (electrical, fluidics, thermal, etc.).

400249

##### MagicLab (Web-based SysML v2 Model Reader)

MagicLab is a new browser-based environment for viewing SysML v2 models. It provides read-only access to projects stored in Teamwork Cloud, eliminating the need to publish or export models for review. Users can open diagrams, tables, and model elements directly in the browser, with changes appearing instantly when the model is updated in the modeling tool or via the SysML v2 REST API. MagicLab also includes a textual representation of the SysML v2 syntax and supports lightweight model sharing across teams without requiring local installation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Integration and Data Synchronization

CATIA DataHub now supports the **Copy Data** operation for **SysML v2 projects**, available for **DOORS**, **DOORS Next (DNG)**, **JAMA Connect**, and **ENOVIA Requirements**connectors, with standard properties such as **Declared Name**and **Documentation Body**included in mapping support.

400249

granted for server projects, ensuring compliance and traceability.</li><li data-uuid="73e89a5a-be77-421a-8d62-99afa9fa5d6c"><strong>Cross-cluster synchronization</strong> – enables model updates to be shared securely across multiple server clusters, facilitating distributed collaboration.</li></ul><p>Integration with 3DEXPERIENCE enables large-scale projects and supports multi-discipline integration (electrical, fluidics, thermal, etc.).</p><p style="text-align: center;"><span class="cke-highlighted"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0d901f2-717a-4087-9d4f-b3145b791ffe"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">400</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x1lLHDEvusk" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p><h3>MagicLab (Web-based SysML v2 Model Reader)</h3><p>MagicLab is a new browser-based environment for viewing SysML v2 models. It provides read-only access to projects stored in Teamwork Cloud, eliminating the need to publish or export models for review. Users can open diagrams, tables, and model elements directly in the browser, with changes appearing instantly when the model is updated in the modeling tool or via the SysML v2 REST API. MagicLab also includes a textual representation of the SysML v2 syntax and supports lightweight model sharing across teams without requiring local installation.<span style="text-align: center;letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) CATIA SysML v2 Solution" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) CATIA SysML v2 Solution" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="418"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLab 3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) CATIA SysML v2 Solution" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Integration and Data Synchronization</h3><p>CATIA DataHub now supports the <strong>Copy Data</strong> operation for <strong>SysML v2 projects</strong>, available for <strong>DOORS</strong>, <strong>DOORS Next (DNG)</strong>, <strong>JAMA Connect</strong>, and <strong>ENOVIA Requirements </strong>connectors, with standard properties such as <strong>Declared Name </strong>and <strong>Documentation Body </strong>included in mapping support.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1520d029-0f4f-4de9-8b09-8936cef30678"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">400</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Jcnk9Xp7CPo" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">249</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093125 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00024: (2026x) Command Palette

- page_id: `298093125`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093125/2026x+Command+Palette
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:06:46.238+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with Textual Editor > (2026x) Textual Editor features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to using commands listed in the Textual Editor shortcut menu, you can use the Command Palette, which contains a multitude of additional commands.You can execute a command by clicking on it in the palette or by using shortcut keys if any are assigned.

##### Invoking the Command Palette

To invoke the Command Palette

Do one of the following:

- Right-click anywhere in the Textual Editor. In the shortcut menu, click the Command Palette command.
- Press F1 on the keyboard.

##### Quick-access symbols table

The usage of the Command Palette and the content displayed in it depends on the first symbol (the quick-access symbol) used in the Command Palette's search field.

| Symbol | Name | Description and Usage |
| --- | --- | --- |
| : | Go to Line/Column | This symbol is used to navigate the cursor to a specific line. Writing the colon in the search field displays the number of the line in which the cursor is positioned at the time. To access a specific line, type its number after the colon, e.g., ':22'.Shortcut keys: Ctrl+G |
| @ | Go to Symbol | This symbol is used to navigate to a navigate to a specific element by its name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation, e.g. '@requirement'. Shortcut keys: Ctrl+Shift+OUse up/down keyboard keys to navigate the displayed search results. Each is highlighted in the textual notation for easy navigation. |
| @: | Go to Symbol by Category | This symbol is used to sort elements by their assigned categories (properties, modules, methods, etc.) |
| > | Show and Run Commands | This symbol is used to display a large selection of various commands you can execute on the textual notation where the cursor is positioned. To search for a specific command type its name after the > symbol, e.g., '>fold'.This is the default quick-access symbol that is displayed in the search field once the Command Palette is displayed. Useful commands'Toggle High Contrast Theme' - switches the editor to dark mode. |
| (empty) | - | If the search field is empty, select the relevant symbol from the displayed menu containing all quick-access symbols. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In the shortcut menu, click the Command Palette command.</li><li>Press F1 on the keyboard.</li></ul><h3>Quick-access symbols table </h3><p>The usage of the Command Palette and the content displayed in it depends on the <u>first</u> symbol (the quick-access symbol) used in the Command Palette's search field.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Symbol</th><th>Name</th><th>Description and Usage</th></tr><tr><td>:</td><td>Go to Line/Column</td><td><p>This symbol is used to navigate the cursor to a specific line. Writing the colon in the search field displays the number of the line in which the cursor is positioned at the time. To access a specific line, type its number after the colon, e.g., ':22'.</p><p><strong>Shortcut keys:</strong> Ctrl+G</p></td></tr><tr><td>@</td><td>Go to Symbol</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>This symbol is used to navigate to a navigate to a specific element by its name. Type the element name you want to search for after the '@' symbol to navigate to the searched element in the textual notation, e.g. '@requirement'. </p><p><strong>Shortcut keys: </strong>Ctrl+Shift+O</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68742d22-1498-4fca-b08a-606f64a9f7a0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use up/down keyboard keys to navigate the displayed search results. Each is highlighted in the textual notation for easy navigation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>@:</td><td>Go to Symbol by Category</td><td><p>This symbol is used to sort elements by their assigned categories (properties, modules, methods, etc.)</p></td></tr><tr><td>&gt;</td><td>Show and Run Commands</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>This symbol is used to display a large selection of various commands you can execute on the textual notation where the cursor is positioned.
