<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093277 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00026: (2026x) Compartments

- page_id: `298093277`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093277/2026x+Compartments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:04:23.609+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View kinds > (2026x) Graphical views > (2026x) Symbolic views > (2026x) Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A compartment view usage is an area on the element symbol that displays specific element data. You can also use compartments to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P']. Any usage or definition element may have compartments listing theirownedand inherited elements. The element symbol compartments are a graphical display of the element body in the textual notation.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

requirementdefMassLimitationRequirement{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be less than or equal to the required mass. */ 
attributemassActual:MassValue; 
attributemassReqd:MassValue; 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=massReqd 
} 
}

###### On the left: the requirement symbol with *documentation*, *attributes*, and *require constraints* compartments. 
On the right: the same requirement with its owned elements declared in the body.

##### Displaying and suppressing compartments

Many symbols have various compartments available to them which are hidden by default. Follow the the options listed below to check it if the needed compartment is not displayed.

To display or suppress compartments, do one of the following

- In the view, click the symbol, then click the **Compartments**button. Select or deselect the particular compartment. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the view, right-click the symbol and in its shortcut menu, click Symbol Style . Then enable or disable the Show Compartment property for the needed compartment in the **Symbol Style**dialog .
- In the view, select a symbol and click the Suppress <compartment name> button. [ATTACHMENT filename='suppress_compartment.png']

##### Displaying or hiding elements in compartments

Use the **Compartment Edit**dialog to control the list of elements and properties visible in symbol compartments.

To open the **Compartment Edit** dialog

- Select a symbol and click the Compartments button. In the submenu, click the arrow to expand and then click Edit Compartments . [ATTACHMENT filename='compartment_edit_dialog.png']

##### Creating elements via compartments

You can use compartments to create new owned elements for the selected element.

To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_an_element_via_the_element_symbol_in_a_diagram.png']
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the compartment of the element kind you want to create, click the Create <element kind> button. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_an_element_via_compartment_in_a_diagram.png']

##### Compartments table

The table below lists the compartments available to specific element symbols based on their element metaclass.

| Compartment | Element metaclass |  | Compartment | Element metaclass |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| metadata | All namespaces |  | perfom actions | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionStateDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageStateUsage |
| doc | PackageDefinitionsUsages, excluding:IfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |  | subject | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| actors | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | stakeholders | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| objective | CaseUsageCaseDefinition |  | include use cases | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageExhibitStateUsageIfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |
| parameters | ConstraintUsageConstraintDefinitionActionUsageActionDefinition |  | frames | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |
| satisfy requirements | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |  | exhibit states | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| require constraints | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | attributes | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| assume constraints | RequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | items | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages |
| assert constraints | PartUsagePartDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | enums | EnumerationDefinition |
| constraints | CaseUsageCaseDefinitionRequirementUsageRequirementDefinition |  | actions | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsage |
| parts | Definitions, excluding:EnumDefinitionUsages, excluding: EnumUsageIfActionUsageWhileActionUsageForActionUsage |  | ends | ConnectorAsUsageConnectionDefinition |
| ports | PartUsagePartDefinitionPortUsagePortDefinition |  | members | Package |
| while condition | WhileActionUsage |  | features (name not displayed in compartment) | MetadataUsage |
| until condition | WhileActionUsage |  | rules | Part usage or definition specializing Policies::StyleSheet |
| for iterator | ForLoopActionUsage |  |  |  |
| if condition | IfActionUsageElseAction |  |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093616 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00027: (2026x) Concerns

- page_id: `298093616`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093616/2026x+Concerns
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-13T13:49:46.936+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A concern is used to specify the concerns of one or more stakeholders.One or more concerns can then be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Frame concern' space='UAF2P'] in other requirement definitions and usages.

##### Creating concern usage and definition elements

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *concern def*for a concern definition.
  2. *concern* for a concern usage.
2. Name the element.
3. Specify the element in the element body. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space='UAF2P'] page.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

concerndefClientConcerns{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
stakeholderclient:Clients; 
doc/* Client concerns regarding the vehicle mass, functionality, and design. */ 
} 
concernmassConcern:ClientConcerns{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
stakeholderclient1:Clients; 
requireconstraint{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be equal to the required mass. */ 
} 
}

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. concern def to create a concern definition element.
  2. concern to create a concern usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the concern. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the procedures on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create concern usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **concern** def button to create a concern definition element.
  2. The **concern** button to create a concern usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **concern**or the **concern****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the concern symbol.
3. Specify the concern. Concerns can be specified with the same elements used for requirement specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space='UAF2P'] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093694 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00028: (2026x) Connection

- page_id: `298093694`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093694/2026x+Connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:54:23.552+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A connection definition is a relationship that classifies a connection between related things, such as items and parts. A connection usage relates features to connector ends from the connection definition via a reference subsetting relationship, associating those ends with the specific usage elements that are to be connected. Connections can be either binary, with two connection ends, or n-ary, with multiple ends. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) N-ary connection' space='UAF2P'] page.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='UAF2P'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *pressureSeatConn* connection connector where *bead* reference subsets *t.bead* connecting to *mountingRim*which reference subsets *w.rim*. 
Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P'].

##### Creating connection usage and definition elements

###### Creating connection usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create connection usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'].
3. Click the Synchronize button. connectiondefPressureSeat{ 
endpartbead:TireBead; 
endpartmountingRim:TireMountingRim; 
} 
partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partt:Tire[1] { 
partbead:TireBead[2]; 
} 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
}// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:connectt.beadtow.rim;// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list:connect(t.bead,w.rim); 
// unnamed unspecialized connection usage with named ends connecting featuresin a comma-separated list:connect(beadEnd ::>t.bead,rimEnd ::>w.rim);// unnamed specialized (feature typing) connection usage connecting featureswith explicit declaration: connection:PressureSeatconnectt.beadtow.rim;// named unspecialized connection usage connecting features with explicit declaration:connectionpressureSeatConnconnectt.beadtow.rim;// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting featureswith explicit declaration:connectionpressureSeatConn: PressureSeat connect bead ::> t.bead to mountingRim ::> w.rim; 
// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list:connectionpressureSeatConn:PressureSeatconnect(bead::>t.bead,mountingRim::>w.rim);} 
}

###### Creating connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the connection command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via a connection. A connection path is created between the elements.

###### Creating connection usage elements via the Create Relationship command

To create connection usage elements via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click connection . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Creating connection usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create connection usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create a connection definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **connection** def button to create a connection definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the connection definition symbol to be created.
- To create a connection usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **connection** button to create a connection usage.
  2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. A connection symbol is created between the elements. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection, n-ary connection**or the **connection****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating connection definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create connection definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword connection def .
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] of the needed element.
3. Click the Synchronize button. connectiondefPressureSeat{ 
endpartbead:TireBead; 
endpartmountingRim:TireMountingRim; 
}

###### Creating connection definition elements via the Create Element command

To create connection definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click connection def for connection definition.

###### Auto-creating connection definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create a connection definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A connection definition is a relationship that classifies a connection between related things, such as items and parts. A connection usage relates features to connector ends from the connection definition via a reference subsetting relationship, associating those ends with the specific usage elements that are to be connected. Connections can be either binary, with two connection ends, or n-ary, with multiple ends. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) N-ary connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[N-ary connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17973459-957d-4107-8aaa-02a3ac9cbe62"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The kind keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Cross features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_connection.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Connection" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The image displays a <em>pressureSeatConn</em> connection connector where <em>bead</em> reference subsets <em>t.bead</em> connecting to <em>mountingRim </em>which reference subsets <em>w.rim</em>.<br />Additionally, the connector is also <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></h6><h3>Creating connection usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating connection usage elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">connection</em><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword.</span></li><li>Specify the element name (optional),<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>(optional), then state the features explicitly (using the <em>connect...to </em>syntax<span>) </span>or in a comma-separated list of related features.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0af6619-dda5-4ca5-8208-158cc9996ff5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>connection<span> </span></em>can be omitted.</li><li>You can also name connection ends, <span>associating them to features via </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>connection </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via a connection. A connection path is created between the elements.</li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage elements <span>via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p>To create connection usage elements via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>connection</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Connection" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Creating connection usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create connection usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create a connection definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>connection </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create a connection definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the connection definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create a connection usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>connection </strong></span><span>button to create a connection usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. A connection symbol is created between the elements.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>connection, n-ary connection </strong></span>or the <span><strong>connection </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4>Creating connection definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create connection definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword<span> </span><em>connection def</em><span>.</span></li><li>Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the needed element. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00fd817-e309-4d8e-839d-5b21596e9da9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PressureSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireBead</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span>end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating connection definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create connection definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create a connection definition element via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093492 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00030: (2026x) Constraints

- page_id: `298093492`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093492/2026x+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-13T15:40:34.829+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A constraint definition defines a logical condition, and a constraint usage applies this condition in different contexts, enabling the reuse of the constraint definition throughout the system. The constraint usage is satisfied when its expression evaluates to true, and violated when it does not.

##### Creating constraint usage and definition elements

###### Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create constraint usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. constraint def for a constraint definition.
  2. *constraint* for a constraint usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.) in the element's body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

constraintdefMassLimit{ // constraint definition 
attributemass:MassValue; 
attributemassLimit:MassValue; 
mass<=massLimit 
}

partvehicle{ 
attribute massActual : MassValue; 
attributemassRequired:MassValue;

constraintmassConstraint { massActual<=massRequired }// named constraint usage 
constraint{ massActual<=massRequired }// unnamed constraint usage 
}

###### Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create constraint usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. constraint def to create a constraint definition element.
  2. constraint to create a constraint usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).

###### Creating constraint usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create constraint usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **constraint** def button to create a constraint definition element.
  2. The **constraint** button to create a constraint usage element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the constraint.
3. Specify the constraint (expression, attributes, etc.).

###### Auto-creating constraint definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create constraint definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093821 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00031: (2026x) Control nodes

- page_id: `298093821`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093821/2026x+Control+nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:57:55.729+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Controlling actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The sequencing of action usages is controlled using control nodes. Control nodes are always connected to other action usages by incoming and outgoing successions (with or without guards). Kinds of control nodes include the following:

- Fork node
  - Has one incoming succession and one or more outgoing successions.
  - The actions connected to the outgoing successions cannot start until the action connected to the incoming succession has completed.
- Join node
  - Has one or more incoming successions and one outgoing succession.
  - The action connected to the outgoing succession cannot start until all the actions connected to the incoming successions have completed.
- Decision node
  - Has one incoming succession and one or more outgoing successions.
  - Once the action connected to the incoming succession has completed, exactly one of the actions connected to an outgoing succession can start based on the guard condition of the outgoing succession that is satisfied (the else condition is the complement of all other guard conditions). See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Guard conditions' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Merge node
  - Has one or more incoming successions and one outgoing succession.
  - The action connected to the outgoing succession cannot start until any one of the actions connected to an incoming succession has completed.

##### Creating control nodes

###### Creating control nodes via the Textual Editor

To create control nodes via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *fork*for a fork node.
  2. join for a join node.
  3. decide for a decision node
  4. merge for a merge node.
2. (Optional) Specify the node name .
3. Click the Synchronize button. actiontransportPassenger{ 
firststart; 
thenactionenterVehicle; 
thenactionstartVehicle; 
thenmergeNode; 
mergemergeNode;// named merge node declaration; it waits for the 'startVehicle'action to happen before continuingthenforkNode; 
forkforkNode;// namedfork node declaration; it enables all its successors to happen after it 
thenactiondriveVehicle; // the source of this succession is the fork node 
thenactionprovidePower;// the source of this succession is the fork node 
thenjoinNode; 
thenjoinNode; 
joinjoinNode;// namedjoin node declaration; it waits for all its predecessors to happen before continuing 
thenactionexitVehicle; 
thendecideNode; 
decidedecideNode; // nameddecide node declaration; typically followed by one or more conditional successions from which it chooses exactly one to happen after it 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // references the merge node named 'mergeNode' as the target of the succession 
elsedone; 
}

###### Creating control nodes via the view palette

To create control nodes via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. **fork**for a fork node.
  2. join for a join node.
  3. decide for a decision node
  4. merge for a merge node.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the node symbol.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The sequencing of action usages is controlled using control nodes. Control nodes are always connected to other action usages by incoming and outgoing successions (with or without guards). Kinds of control nodes include the following:</p><ul><li>Fork node<ul><li>Has one incoming succession and one or more outgoing successions.</li><li>The actions connected to the outgoing successions cannot start until the action connected to the incoming succession has completed.</li></ul></li><li>Join node<br /><ul><li>Has one or more incoming successions and one outgoing succession. </li><li>The action connected to the outgoing succession cannot start until all the actions connected to the incoming successions have completed.</li></ul></li><li>Decision node<ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Has one incoming succession and one or more outgoing successions.</span></li><li>Once the action connected to the incoming succession has completed, exactly one of the actions connected to an outgoing succession can start based on the guard condition of the outgoing succession that is satisfied (the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">else</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> condition is the complement of all other guard conditions). See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Guard conditions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Guard conditions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Merge node</span><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Has one or more incoming successions and one outgoing succession. </span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The action connected to the outgoing succession cannot start until any one of the actions connected to an incoming succession has completed.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Creating control nodes</h3><h4>Creating control nodes via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create control nodes via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><em>fork </em>for a fork node.</p></li><li><em>join </em>for a join node.</li><li><em>decide </em>for a decision node</li><li><em>merge </em>for a merge node.</li></ol></li><li><span>(Optional) Specify the node name</span>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="556914bd-c697-465d-8375-6e3c6e2d2133"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span>{</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">start</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span> </span></span><span 0.0px;">control nodes via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>fork </strong>for a fork node.</p></li><li><strong>join </strong>for a join node.</li><li><strong>decide </strong>for a decision node</li><li><strong>merge </strong>for a merge node.</li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the node symbol.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093817 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: (2026x) Controlling actions

- page_id: `298093817`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093817/2026x+Controlling+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:57:48.257+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can control actions using the following elements:

- **Start**and **done**actions that allow you to specify the start and end of an action sequence.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Start and done actions' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Control nodes , such as decision, merge, fork, and join nodes that allow you to control the sequencing of action usages.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Control nodes' space='UAF2P'] page.
- **Guard conditions**that allow you to specify conditional succession.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Guard conditions' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Structured control actions , such as if action usage, while action usage, for action usage ****that allow you to control the performance of nested action usages in a structured way.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Structured control actions' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>You can control actions using the following elements:</span></p><ul><li><span><strong>Start </strong>and <strong>done </strong>actions that <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">allow you to specify the start and end of an action sequence.</span></span><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Start and done actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Start and done actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></span></li></ul></li><li><strong>Control nodes</strong>, such as decision, merge, fork, and join nodes that allow you to control the sequencing of action usages.<ul><li>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Control nodes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Control nodes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li><li><span><strong>Guard conditions </strong>that allow you to specify conditional succession.</span><ul><li><span>See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Guard conditions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Guard conditions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></li><li><strong>Structured control actions</strong>, such as if action usage, while action usage, for action usage<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span><strong> </strong>that allow you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">control the performance of nested action usages in a structured way.</span></span></span><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Structured control actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Structured control actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></span></span></li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093809 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00033: (2026x) Creating actions

- page_id: `298093809`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093809/2026x+Creating+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:57:09.389+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An action definition is a kind of occurrence definition that defines action performances. An action usage is a kind of occurrence usage that is a usage of one or more action definitions performed in a specific context. Actions coordinate the performance of other actions and generate effects on items and parts involved in the performance.

##### Creating action usage and definition elements

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create action usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *action def*for an action definition.
  2. action for an action usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) Specify the action's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

actiondefTakePicture;// action definition

actiontakePicture:TakePicture;// action usage

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create action usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. action def to create an action definition element.
  2. action to create an action usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the action's features.

###### Creating action usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create action usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **action** def button to create an action definition element.
  2. The **action** button to create an action usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **action**or the **action def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the action symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the action's features.

###### Creating action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an action symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the action element.
  - Select an action symbol and on the right side of the actions compartment, click the Create action button. The new element is created and displayed in the actions compartment.

###### Creating action usage elements via the Specification panel

To create action usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **action**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **actions**compartment section's bar, click the **Create action**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **actions** compartment.

###### Auto-creating action definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create action definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Auto-creating action usage elements via the Extract Usage command

To auto-create action usage elements via the Extract Usage command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>To create action usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view </p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select an action symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>action </strong>element. </li><li>Select an action symbol and on the right side of the <strong>actions </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>action </span></strong>button. <br />The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>actions </strong>compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating action usage elements via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create action usage elements via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating actions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>action </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>actions </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create action</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating actions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>actions</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating action definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create action definition elements via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Auto-c<span>reating action usage elements via the Extract Usage command</span></h4><p><span>To auto-create action usage elements via the Extract Usage command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094024 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00034: (2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions

- page_id: `298094024`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094024/2026x+Creating+custom+symbolic+view+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-02-26T10:52:36.486+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating symbolic views > (2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom symbolic view definition creation with custom and/or inherited view palettes, and predefined and/or custom templated buttons in custom and/or inherited button categories.

The procedures on this page use the example of creating custom symbolic view definitions by customizing the*DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv***standard predefined symbolic view definition. This is the recommended workflow for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'], as the *Base Symbolic View* definition carries the predefined view palette, which contains Selection, Tools, and Common button categories, as well as the rendering feature, providing a flexible basis for creating custom symbolic view definitions.However, you can similarly customize any other symbolic view definition, whether [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] or custom.

#### NOTE: Updating custom view definition

Updating custom view definition

Reload the project for changes made to the custom symbolic view definition to take effect.

##### Creating custom symbolic view definitions

To create custom symbolic view definitions

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P'] the *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette* package.
5. Declare a view definition, specify the name for the new symbolic view definition, subclassify the Base Symbolic View definition from the DS_Views::CoreViews package, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] within the body of the view definition.
6. Click the Synchronize button. The new view definition becomes available in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] once you:[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'].Add the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']. This requires you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].

package'Custom symbolic view definitions' { // package for the custom symbolic view definition(s)privateimportDS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::*; 
viewdef'Requirements View':>DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ // new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View' subclassifying'bsv', i.e., *'Base Symbolic View'*definition 
// specify the view palette here}}

##### Creating palettes for custom symbolic view definitions

To create palettes for custom symbolic view definitions

1. Within the body of the view definition element of the new symbolic view definition, redefine the baseViewPalette inherited from the Base Symbolic View definition.
2. Specify button categories by doing any of the following:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']
3. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ // new custom symbolic view definition 'Requirements View'part:>>baseViewPalette{ // redefines the *baseViewPalette* inherited from the *Base Symbolic View* definition. The custom palette now inherits Selection, Tools, and Common button categories// specify button categorieshere} 
}

##### Managing button categories in custom symbolic view palettes

The procedures in this subsection explain how to manage inherited and custom button categories and their attributes.

###### Removing inherited button categories

To remove inherited button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button category you want to remove. The button categories that are inherited from the *baseViewPalette* are *selectionCategory*, *toolsCategory*, and *commonCategory*. You can find them in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*package. You can also find the button categories in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].
3. Set the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{

part:>> toolsCategory[0];// unnamed part redefines *toolsCategory*inherited via *baseViewPalette,*specifying the redefining button category's multiplicity to zero to remove it} 
}

###### Adding custom button categories

To add custom button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, and then subset *buttonCategories*.
3. Within the new button category's body, do any of the following:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] .
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories; // new button category instance} 
}

###### Specifying attributes for custom or inherited button categories

To specify attributes for custom or inherited button categories

1. Within the body of the button category element, redefine the attributes that you want to modify with the needed values. All button categories - inherited and custom ones - inherit all the same button category attributes from *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Components::AbstractItem* and *ItemsCategory*, which are: *isCollapsed*, *hasVisibleLabels*, *label*, *description.*You can also find the attributes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{ 
part:>>baseViewPalette{ 
// modifying an inherited button category's attributes: 
part:>>selectionCategory{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Selection";// modified category name 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified category default collapse state 
} 
// specifying a custom button category's attributes: 
partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories{ 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaultfalse;// newly specified *isCollapsed* attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>hasVisibleLabelsdefaulttrue;// newly specified *hasVisibleLabels*attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Requirements";// newly specified *label*attribute for the custom button category 
attribute:>>descriptiondefault"Requirements";// newly specified *description*attribute for the custom button category 
}} 
}

##### Managing buttons in button categories

A button category can contain specific and/or menu buttons that contain specific buttons in its submenu. You can also specify one of the submenu buttons as the focused button that would be displayed as the default one for the menu button. Additionally, you can create templates for custom buttons, allowing you to add custom templated buttons to custom and/or inherited button categories.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. 
The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.

###### Creating menu buttons in button categories

To create menu buttons in button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the button category element*.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *ButtonsCategory,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Add submenu buttons for the menu button:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].
4. (Optional) Specify the focused button:
  1. Redefine *focusedButton*, and specify the focused button by indicating the name of the needed button's part element.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partreqButtonMenu:ButtonsCategory:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
partreqButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button

performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementAction;}} 
partreqDefButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementDefAction;} 
} 
part:>>focusedButton=reqButton; // the part *reqButton* is specified as the focused (default) button for the menu button 
}}} 
}

###### Adding predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

To add predefined buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To create specific buttons, place the cursor within the body of the button category element.
  2. To create submenu buttons for the menu button, place the cursor within the body of the menu button element.
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *Button,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Within the body of the part, declare a perform action, define it by *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode,* and subset *operation*. In the element's body, specify the *in*direction with the code action identifier based on the type of element you want the button to create. You can find the needed code action identifier for the element kind you want the button to create in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers*package. It looks like this:attributerequirementAction : ScalarValues::String="ADD_REQUIREMENT"; // the code action identifier for the requirement usage element button is *requirementAction*
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partreqButtonMenu:ButtonsCategory:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
partreqButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button

performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementAction;} // specifies *requirementAction* so that this button creates the requirement usage element} 
partreqDefButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // submenu button 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromCode:>operation{in= DS_Views::ViewPalettes::CodeActionIdentifiers::requirementDefAction;} // specifies *requirementDefAction* so that this button creates the requirement definition element 
} 
part:>>focusedButton=reqButton; 
}}} 
}

###### Removing buttons from inherited button categories

To remove buttons from inherited button categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the element redefining *baseViewPalette.*
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name (optional), and then redefine the button you want to remove. See the buttons specified for each predefined button category in the *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*package. You can also find it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].
3. Set the redefining button's multiplicity to zero.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{

part:>>selectionCategory{ // unnamed part redefines *selectionCategory*inherited via *baseViewPalette*

// ------ predefined button:partcompleteCoverageModeButton :DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Palette::Button:> abstractButtons

// ------ modified button (instead of the notation above):part:>>completeCoverageModeButton[0]; // multiplicity for the button *completeCoverageModeButton*is set to zero to remove it}} 
}

##### Managing custom templated buttons in button categories

Instead of or in addition to using predefined buttons either by adding them to your custom or inherited button categories, you can also create your own custom buttons. For that, first, you need to create a template for the custom button, and then you can add it to a specific button category of the view palette.

###### Creating templates for custom buttons

The following procedure depicts how to create an element template using metadata to extend the requirement usage element with the needed attribute, where the requirement usage element is named *Software*. Then the template can be passed into the custom button specification using *metadataAccessExpression*.

To create templates for custom buttons

1. Within the package containing the new symbolic view definition, d eclare:
  1. A metadata definition element with the needed name and attributes.
  2. A package element for the template.
    1. Within the template element, apply metadata to the element kind you want to extend and specify the element name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

package 'Custom symbolic view definitions' {

metadatadefSoftware{ // declared metadata definition with the needed attribute 
attributeverificationMethod[0..*] :VerificationMethod; 
} 
packagesoftwareReqTemplate{ // custom button template 
#SoftwarerequirementsoftwareRequirement; // the metadata *Software*is applied to the requirement usage element extending it with a new name *Software*and a metadata attribute. The name *softwareRequirement*will be used for the perform action's element specification to pass the template to a button in a button category}

}

###### Adding custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

To add custom templated buttons to custom or inherited button categories or menu buttons

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To create specific buttons, place the cursor within the body of the button category element.
  2. To create submenu buttons for the menu button, place the cursor within the body of the menu button element.
2. Declare a part usage, specify the element name, define it by *Button,* and then subset *abstractButtons*.
3. Within the body of the part, specify attributes for the custom templated button:
  1. Redefine the label attribute and specify the button name.
  2. Redefine the description attribute and specify the button's tooltip.
4. Declare a perform action, define it by *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate,* and subset *operation*. In the element's body, specify the *in* direction with the custom button's template path using *metadataAccessExpression* . A *metadataAccessExpression* must be used for the *in*element. Its result must be exactly one templated element that you want to copy for the button.Alternatively, you can also use metaclassification expressions, e.g., when the templated element is unnamed. The expression would be: *(**softwareReqTemplate meta KerML::Element).ownedElement*
5. Click the Synchronize button.

viewdef'Requirements View':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bsv{part:>>baseViewPalette{partrequirementsCategory:>buttonCategories {

partsoftwareButton:Button:>abstractButtons{ // menu button instance 
attribute:>>label="software requirement"; // specify the button name 
attribute:>>description="software requirement"; // specify the button tooltip 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>operation{in=softwareReqTemplate::softwareRequirement .metadata; } // *OperationFromTemplate*specifies that the indicated button is templated; *in***specifies the created element, which is *softwareRequirement*(which also contains metadata)**from the template *softwareReqTemplate* 
}}} 
}
