For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']page.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. // the following style sheet contains a global condition 'General View Condition' followed by an individual rule for a specific element metaclass and applicable stylepackageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
 privateimportPredicates::*; 
 privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
 privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:>StyleSheet{ 
/* -------global condition -------*/ 
part'General View Condition':>>styleSheetCondition:ByDiagramTypeCondition{ // global style sheet condition targeting symbols by view definition 
refitem:>>diagramType=DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv.metadata; // the condition targets views defined by the *General View*view definition 
} 
/* -----------------------------------*/ 
partpartRule:>rule{ // an individual rule targets *PartUsage* metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ 
attribute:>>textColordefault"#2A16FF"; 
} 
} 
} 
}

##### Creating custom rules

###### Creating custom rules

To create a custom rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, s pecify the rule, d eclare a part usage, specify a name for the new rule, subset the rule element inherited from the StyleSheet element, and in its body, do any of the following as needed:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. packageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
 privateimportPredicates::*; 
 privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
 privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:> StyleSheet{/* -------new rule -------*/ 
partpartRule:>rule{// Specify the rule's condition 
 // Specify the rule's style 
}/* -----------------------------------*/ 
} 
}

##### Specifying conditions for a rule

###### Specifying conditions for a rule

To specify a condition for a rule

1. Within the body of the part element of the rule, declare a part usage and r edefine the condition element inherited from the StyleSheet element, then define it by the needed condition.
2. Within the body of the condition, declare a ref item , then r edefine the needed item and specify its values.
  1. For the FreeFormCondition , declare a calc and redefine test , then specify the needed expression. The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. // *ByElementMetaclassCondition* examplepartpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{// the rule's condition is specified as *ByElementMetaclassCondition*, targeting symbols by element metaclass 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; // the condition targets elements with the *PartUsage* metaclasses 
} 
}// *ByMetadataAnnotationCondition*examplepart metadataApprovalRule :> rule {part :>> condition : ByMetadataAnnotationCondition {// the rule's condition is specified as *ByMetadataAnnotationCondition*, targeting symbols by element metadata 
 ref item :>> annotatingMetadataType = 'Vehicle Metadata Definitions'::Approval.metadata;// the condition targets elements with the applied *Approval* metadata 
 }}// *ByDiagramTypeCondition*examplepart generalViewRule :> rule {part :>> condition : ByDiagramTypeCondition {// the rule's condition is specified as *ByDiagramTypeCondition*, targeting symbols by view definition 
ref item :>> diagramType = DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv.metadata; // the condition targets views defined by the *General View*view definition 
 }}// *FreeFormCondition*examplepartMyFreeformRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:FreeFormCondition{ // the rule's condition is specified as *FreeFormCondition*, targeting symbols by the specified expression 
calc:>>test{ 
'VCCU View'.metadata==diagram// targets element symbols on the specific view '*VCCU View*' 
 // ORelement== VCCU.metadata// targets the *VCCU* element symbol on all views (to which the style sheet is applied)} 
} 
}

##### Specifying styles for a rule

There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:

- Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] .
- External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for style specification of different rules. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule

To specify style attributes locally for the style of a rule

1. Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and redefine the style element inherited from the StyleSheet element.
2. Within the body of the view element, d eclare an attribute usage, redefine the needed attribute and specify its value. The available attributes depend on the style definitions (primary or custom)used for the view's definition:For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']page.To create custom style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] procedure.To define by multiple style definitions, list them in a comma-separated list.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P'].

part partRule :> rule {part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ // the rule's style is defined by a primary style definition *StyleShape* 
attribute:>>textColordefault"#FF0F37";// local modification of an available attribute's value 
} 
}

###### Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds

To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds

1. Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and redefine the style element inherited from the StyleSheet element.
2. Within the body of the view element, d eclare an attribute usage and apply the InvisibleInDialog metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition . The available attributes depend on the style definitions (primary or custom)used for the view's definition.For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']page.For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space='UAF2P'] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P'].

part partRule :> rule {part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ 
#InvisibleInDialogattribute:>>contentFillColor; // the style attribute *Content Fill Color* is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages} 
}

###### Creating custom style definitions

Unlike local attribute specification, creating custom style definitions allows you to reuse them for style specification of multiple rules.

You can create custom style definitions specifying the needed attributes and their values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] must subclassify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] (such as *ShapeStyle, ShapeOnBorderStyle*, etc.), inheriting their attributes, which you can modify. However, you can only add attributes that are available to the primary style definition that the custom style definition subclassifies. 
For an extensive list of primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create a custom style definition

1. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, d eclare a view definition element, specify its name, and subclassify a primary style definition from the DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles package. For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']page.
2. Within the body of the view definition element, d eclare an attribute usage, r edefine the needed attribute and specify the attribute's value. The available attributes and their value types depend on the primary style definition you are using.For an extensive list of the primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P']page.
3. Specify the custom style definition for the style of a rule.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P'].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>StyleSheet{ 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ // custom rule 
ref item :>> elementMetaclass = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view : PartTextStyleRule :>> style; // the style of the rule is updated to be defined by the new custom style definition 
} 
viewdefPartTextStyleRule:>ShapeStyle{ // new custom style definition subclassifies the primary style definition *ShapeStyle* 
attribute :>> textColor default "#68FF70"; 
} 
}

###### Creating custom reusable style attribute values

You can create custom style attributes whose values you can reuse by referencing them where needed. Additionally, you can create custom style classes grouping such attributes.

To create custom reusable style attribute values

1. Declare an attribute, s pecify its name, then define it by the needed value type, and s pecify its value.
2. (Optional) To group attributes within a custom style class, declare a part usage, specify its name, then place the attributes within the part's body.
3. Reuse the newly created style attribute values for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] . Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] for attributes nested within [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P'].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>StyleSheet{ 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:PartTextStyleRule:>>style; // the custom style is passed to the rule's style 
} 
viewdefPartTextStyleRule:>ShapeStyle{ 
attribute:>>penColordefaultcustomColorPalette.customDarkGreen; // the custom color from a custom style class is reused in a custom style definition 
} 
partcustomColorPalette{// custom style class with reusable style attributes for custom color values are specified 
attributecustomDarkGreen:Colordefault"#357F35"; 
attributecustomLightGreen:Colordefault"#BFF7AA"; 
} 
}

##### Specifying the compartment style of a rule

To specify the compartment style of a rule

1. Within the body of the view element, declare a part usage, specify its name (optional), then d efine it by the appropriate compartment style definition. To determine which primary compartment style definitions are available for each element symbol, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.
2. Within the body, redefine the needed compartment style attribute followed by the appropriate attribute value. **** To determine which attributes and what value types are available to each primary compartment style definition, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ // defined bya primary style definition 
attributeCompartment :AttributesCompartmentStyle{ // attribute for the attributes compartment's 
:>>showCompartmentdefaulttrue; // modifying the *showCompartment* value 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>See the procedures on this page to create style sheet components.</p><h2>Creating custom style sheet components</h2><h3>Creating global conditions for a style sheet</h3><p>In addition to specifying conditions for individual rules in the style sheet (see procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link>), you can specify a global condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the global rule.</p><h4>Creating global conditions for a style sheet</h4><p>To create global conditions for a style sheet</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, redefine the <em>styleSheetCondition </em>element inherited from </span>the <em>StyleSheet </em>element<em> </em>and define it by the needed condition.</li><li>Within the body of the condition, declare a <em>ref item</em>, then r<span>edefine the needed item </span><span>and specify its values.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5053b1bb-6225-446a-ad8d-a19ee76837c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet rule conditions table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet rule conditions table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet conditions and styles tables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7ba2d36-872c-4fcb-8846-b906891989bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the following style sheet contains a global condition 'General View Condition' followed by an individual rule for a specific element metaclass and applicable style</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStylesPackage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreStylesComponents</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Predicates</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColorDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerMLStyles</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysMLStyles</span>::*;</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* -------<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">global condition -------<span> </span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'General View Condition'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">styleSheetCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByDiagramTypeCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// global style sheet condition targeting symbols by view definition</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagramType</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets views defined by the <em>General View </em>view definition</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------<span> </span></span>*/ </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// an individual rule targets <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#2A16FF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Creating custom rules</h3><h4>Creating custom rules</h4><p>To create a custom rule</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, s</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">pecify the rule, d</span>eclare a part usage, specify a name for the new rule, subset the <em>rule </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element, and in its body, do <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">any of the following as needed:</span><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the rule's condition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying styles for a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the rule's style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3462d38f-58d9-4205-935c-b42fd15cadd7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStylesPackage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreStylesComponents</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Predicates</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColorDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerMLStyles</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysMLStyles</span>::*;</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* -------<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">new rule <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------</span></span><span> </span></span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">part</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">partRule</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c"> :&gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">ru</ac:inline-comment-marker>le</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Specify the rule's condition</span><br />               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Specify the rule's style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------</span></span><span> </span></span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying conditions for a rule</h3><h4>Specifying conditions for a rule</h4><p>To specify a condition for a rule</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, declare a part usage and r</span>edefine the <em>condition </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet</em> element, then define it by the needed condition.</li><li>Within the body of the condition, declare a <em>ref item</em>, then r<span>edefine the needed item </span><span>and specify its values.</span><ol><li data-uuid="324224b0-6b91-4f31-8bff-0fc1177b7e1d">For the <em>FreeFormCondition</em>, declare a <em>calc </em>and redefine <em>test</em>, then specify the needed expression.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66f6e464-a7c3-442a-a23a-47fbe9442747"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet rule conditions table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet rule conditions table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54ec3c1e-f836-4456-9c0a-556a564fff5c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em> example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metaclass </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets elements with the <em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">metadataApprovalRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metadata</span></span><br />      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">annotatingMetadataType</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Vehicle Metadata Definitions'</span>::Approval.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets elements with the applied <em>Approval</em> metadata</span></span></span><br />   }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">generalViewRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByDiagramTypeCondition</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition</em>, targeting symbols by view definition</span></span><br /><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagramType</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets views defined by the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>General View </em>view definition</span></span></span></span><br />   }<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>FreeFormCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MyFreeformRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FreeFormCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>FreeFormCondition</em>, targeting symbols by the specified expression</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         '</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">VCCU View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets element symbols on the specific view '<em>VCCU View</em>'<br />         // OR<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">         element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == VCCU.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets the <em>VCCU</em> element symbol on all views (to which the style sheet is applied)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      <br />      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying styles for a rule</h3><p>There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:</p><ul><li>Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link>.</li><li>External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for style specification of different rules. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><h4>Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule</h4><p>To specify style attributes locally for the style of a rule</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and </span>redefine the <em>style </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element.</li><li><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, </span><span>d</span>eclare an attribute usage, redefine the needed attribute and <span>specify its value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2c2a0ef-0685-478d-9e4a-5f70ded74929"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the style definitions <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(primary or custom) </span>used for the view's definition:</span><ul><li>For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></li><li>To create custom style definitions, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link> procedure.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To define by multiple style definitions, list them in a comma-separated list.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce17c2fa-38b1-4524-9b03-d6abee3ebde3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's style is <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">defined by a primary style definition <em>StyleShape</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FF0F37&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// local modification of an available attribute's value</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds</h4><p>To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8cf9a6dd-9439-4468-a94d-d3a142be4436"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and </span>redefine the <em>style </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element.</li><li data-uuid="8d40055e-33d0-470c-abcf-c70e4f0ea1d4"><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, </span><span>d</span>eclare an attribute usage and apply the <em>InvisibleInDialog </em>metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition<span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="922934b4-4737-4b7b-a1b2-9b180f221f01"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the style definitions <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(primary or custom) </span>used for the view's definition. </span>For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</li><li data-uuid="14d743eb-5b86-41e9-86f0-1a15c1a8cbec">For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4d20e455-c815-4318-87bc-6fe4f415e4f9"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18071fed-1f01-4d4f-98fd-7271fa24e1ba"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rule</span> {</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">     </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">#</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">InvisibleInDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">contentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the style attribute <em>Content Fill Color</em> is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages<br /></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom style definitions</h4><p>Unlike local attribute specification, creating custom style definitions allows you to reuse them for style specification of multiple rules. </p><p>You can create custom style definitions specifying the needed attributes and their values. <ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet composition"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must subclassify <ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet composition"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[primary style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (such as <em>ShapeStyle, ShapeOnBorderStyle</em>, etc.), inheriting their attributes, which you can modify. However, you can only add attributes that are available to the primary style definition that the custom style definition subclassifies. <br />For an extensive list of primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p>To create a custom style definition</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, d</span>eclare a view definition element, specify its name, and subclassify a primary style definition<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> from the </span><em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">package. </span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="596add9c-8bf9-4461-ba72-634bce333c7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the view definition element, d</span><span>eclare an attribute usage, r</span><span>edefine the needed attribute and specify the attribute's value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41ce5236-0257-4e7a-a387-75d00f1383a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available attributes and their value types depend on the primary style definition you are using. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For an extensive list of the primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specify the custom style definition for the style of a rule.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8008acdb-37fd-4acc-aea6-817d5ce97e9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">custom rule</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span> : <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the style of the rule is updated to be defined by the new custom style definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">custom style definition subclassifies the primary style definition <em>ShapeStyle</em></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#68FF70&quot;</span>;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom reusable style attribute values</h4><p style="text-align: left;">You can create custom style attributes whose values you can reuse by referencing them where needed. Additionally, you can create custom style classes grouping such attributes. </p><p>To create custom reusable style attribute values</p><hr /><ol><li><span>Declare an attribute, s</span>pecify its name, then define it by the needed value type, and s<span>pecify its value. </span></li><li>(Optional) To group attributes within a custom style class, declare a part usage, specify its name, then place the attributes within the part's body.</li><li>Reuse the newly created style attribute values for <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom style definition attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local attribute specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link>. <span>Use <ac:link ac:anchor="Name resolution"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature chaining]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for attributes nested within <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom reusable style attribute values"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom style classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /></ac:link>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78d7dca6-fd32-48a1-8bdc-0a91345f6019"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part </span>partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the custom style is passed to the rule's style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customColorPalette.customDarkGreen</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the custom color from a custom style class is reused in a custom style definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customColorPalette</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// custom style class with reusable style attributes for custom color values are specified</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customDarkGreen</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#357F35&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;      </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customLightGreen</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#BFF7AA&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Specifying the compartment style of a rule</h3><p>To specify the compartment style of a rule </p><hr /><ol><li><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, d<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">eclare a part usage, specify its name (optional), then d</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">efine it by the appropriate compartment style definition. </span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8c8880-e28e-45bb-9747-60e585fc5aca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which primary compartment style definitions are available for each element symbol, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol compartment styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol compartment styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body, redefine the needed compartment style attribute followed by the appropriate attribute value. <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong> <br /></strong></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="351a66c1-c268-4e8d-9290-29bd90f9908a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which attributes and what value types are available to each primary compartment style definition, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol compartment styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol compartment styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b872d7c9-2193-4f1f-b8bb-a09d36e490e0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// defined by<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> a primary style definition</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">attributeCompartment <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AttributesCompartmentStyle</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{ <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute for the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attributes compartment's </span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modifying the <em>showCompartment</em> value</span></span><br /><span
````
