style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094117 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00040: (2026x) Creating style sheets

- page_id: `298094117`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094117/2026x+Creating+style+sheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:37:06.545+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles > (2026x) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create custom rule-based symbol styles by creating a style sheet. However, there are two ways you can do that:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] This option allows you to create your own custom rules as well as reuse and customize all the rules already available in the reused style sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., *DefaultStyleSheet*) or a custom-created one. For example, you can reuse the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet, then customize its rules by changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the DefaultStyle*Sheet* 's rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the DefaultStyle*Sheet* .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] This option allows you to create your own custom rules only without reusing any predefined or custom style sheets and their rules . Choose this option if you prefer creating your own rules without the need to reuse or customize the existing ones.

##### Creating a style sheet

###### Reusing an existing style sheet

To reuse an existing style sheet

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P'] the DS_Styles , CoreStylesComponents, Predicates , ColorDefinitions , CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions , KerMLStyles , and SysMLStyles packages. To learn more about each package, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] page.
5. Declare a part definition, specify the name for the new style sheet, subclassify the part definition of the existing style sheet you want to reuse (e.g., DefaultStyleSheet ). You can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] within the body of the new part definition.

packageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
privateimportPredicates::*; 
privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
// specify custom rules or customize those in the *DefaultStyleSheet* style sheet here 
} 
}

###### Creating a custom style sheet from scratch

To create a custom style sheet from scratch

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing and filtering elements' space='UAF2P'] the DS_Styles , CoreStylesComponents, Predicates , ColorDefinitions , CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions , KerMLStyles , and SysMLStyles packages. To learn more about each package, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space='UAF2P'] page.
5. Declare a part definition with the part def keyword, specify the name for the new style sheet, subclassify the StyleSheet part definition. You can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] within the body of the new part definition.

package NewStylesPackage {privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
privateimportPredicates::*; 
privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
privateimportSysMLStyles::*;part def NewStyleSheet :>StyleSheet{ 
 // specify custom rules here } 
}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094014 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00041: (2026x) Creating symbolic views

- page_id: `298094014`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094014/2026x+Creating+symbolic+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:33:12.435+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Review the procedures for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] symbolic views. For basic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views basics' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Creating symbolic views

###### Creating symbolic views via the Create View command

The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:

- Standard predefined symbolic view definitions. See the table on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P']page for a full list.
  - To use predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] procedure.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']

To create symbolic views via the Create View command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element that would be the owner of the new symbolic view.
2. In the shortcut menu, click **Create View**. In the submenu, click the appropriate symbolic view. 
The view is created as a *view usage* element and named after its owner. If the **Create View** command is invoked for:package or namespace - an empty view is created.usage or definition element - a view is created with a docked element symbol representing the element for which the view wascreated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Docked symbols' space='UAF2P']>]]>

###### Creating symbolic views via the Textual Editor

A rendered view is produced when a view usage is defined by a view definition that carries a renderer. For symbolic views, use:

- Predefined symbolic view definitions. See the table on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']

The following procedure exemplifies the creation of predefined symbolic views.

To create symbolic views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.
2. Declare the view keyword, specify the element name, and then define it by the needed view definition.
3. Click the Synchronize button. // new view elements are defined by standard predefined symbolic view definitions:view'General View':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv; // new view element is defined by the 'gv' i.e., view definition 'General View'view'Action Flow View': DS_Views::SymbolicViews::afv; // new view element is defined by the 'afv' i.e., view definition 'Action Flow View' 
view'Interconnection View': DS_Views::SymbolicViews::iv; // new view element is defined by the 'iv' i.e., view definition 'Interconnection View'view'State Transition View': DS_Views::SymbolicViews::stv;// new view element is defined by the 'stv' i.e., view definition 'State Transition View'// new view elements are defined by predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:view'Usages Nested View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::UsagesNestedView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'UsagesNestedView' 
view'Parts Nested View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsNestedView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'PartsNestedView' 
view'Parts & Ports Nested View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::'Parts&PortsNestedView';// new view element is defined by view definition 'Parts&PortsNestedView' 
view'Parts Tree View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsTreeView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'PartsTreeView' 
view'Actions Nested View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::ActionsNestedView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'ActionsNestedView' 
view'Actions Tree View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::ActionsTreeView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'ActionsTreeView' 
view'Requirements Tree View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::RequirementsTreeView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'RequirementsTreeView' 
view'States Nested View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::StatesNestedView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'StatesNestedView' 
view'Tree View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::TreeView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'TreeView' 
view'Nested View':DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NestedView;// new view element is defined by view definition 'NestedView'

###### Creating undefinedsymbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor

Instead of creating symbolic views defined by symbolic view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

- See the table of available renderers on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create undefined views using renderers via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.
2. Declare the view keyword, and specify the element name.
3. In the body of the view element, declare the render keyword followed by the needed renderer.
4. Click the Synchronize button. viewnewView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definition 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; // the view has a renderer *asInterconnectionDiagram*, rendering it a symbolic view with nested-view rendering style 
} 
view newView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definition 
renderViews::asTreeDiagram; // the view has a renderer *asTreeDiagram*, rendering it a symbolic view with tree-view rendering style 
}

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094027 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00042: (2026x) Creating tabular views

- page_id: `298094027`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094027/2026x+Creating+tabular+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:32:25.102+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Review the procedures for creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] tabular views. For basic view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views basics' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Creating tabular views

###### Creating tabular views via the Create View command

The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:

- Standard predefined tabular view definitions. See the table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P']

To create tabular views via the Create View command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element that would be the owner of the new tabular view.
2. In the shortcut menu, click **Create View**. In the submenu, click the appropriate tabular view. 
The view is created as a *view usage* element and named after its owner.

###### Creating tabular views via the Textual Editor

A rendered view is produced when a view usage is defined by a view definition that carries a renderer. For tabular views, use:

- Standard predefined tabular view definitions. See the table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P']page for a full list.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P']

To create tabular views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.
2. Declare the view keyword, specify the element name, and then define it by the needed view definition.
3. Click the Synchronize button. view'Generic Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt; // new view element is defined by the 'gt' i.e., tabular view definition 'Generic Table'view'Requirements Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::rt; // new view element is defined by the 'rt' i.e., tabular view definition 'Requirements Table'

###### Creating undefinedtabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor

Instead of creating tabular views defined by tabular view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

- See the table of available renderers on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create undefined views using renderers via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.
2. Declare the view keyword, and specify the element name.
3. In the body of the view element, declare the render keyword followed by the needed renderer.
4. Click the Synchronize button. view newView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definitionrenderDS_Views::CoreViews::asTable;// the view has a renderer *asTable*, rendering it as a tabular view}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Review the procedures for creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendered]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> tabular views. For basic view creation procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Views basics" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Views basics]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><h3>Creating tabular views </h3><h4>Creating tabular views via the Create View command</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Create View command invokes the View Creation dialog/submenu, which displays the following view definitions:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="7e2faee8-f8ea-4dff-b83a-de87fb4356e8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Standard predefined tabular view definitions. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the table on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>page for a full list.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="683053fb-343d-4bc9-8fcf-7e00cc4a4ef5"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>To create tabular views via the Create View command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element that would be the owner of the new tabular view.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create View</strong>. In the submenu, click the appropriate tabular view.<br />The view is created as a <em>view usage</em> element and named after its owner.</p></li></ol><h4>Creating tabular views via the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">A rendered view is produced when a view usage is defined by a view definition that carries a renderer. For tabular views, use:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="c03e3b88-2573-4a0b-b638-5adfbf162c6a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Standard predefined tabular view definitions. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the table on the <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>page for a full list.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="fe38511a-6719-4c17-a782-ea3768fe0bc5"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>To create tabular views via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.</li><li>Declare the <em>view</em> keyword, specify the element name, and then define it by the needed view definition.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7b8ef13-a838-4639-9257-4194b43a0244"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;           <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view element is defined by the 'gt' i.e., tabular view definition 'Generic Table'<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"><br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Requirements Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::rt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view element is defined by the 'rt' i.e., tabular view definition 'Requirements Table' </span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating <span>undefined </span><span>tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Instead of creating tabular views defined by tabular view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify the </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">renderer directly for the new view.<br /></span></p><ul><li>See the table of available renderers on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Rendering views]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul><p>To create undefined views using renderers via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create a view.</li><li>Declare the <em>view</em> keyword, and specify the element name.</li><li>In the body of the view element, declare the <em>render </em>keyword followed by the needed renderer.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3afcbf43-2cb9-47b1-b77e-5555f5ef1f13"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view element is not defined by any view definition<br /></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">DS_Views::CoreViews::asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the view has a renderer <em>asTable</em>, rendering it as a tabular view</span></span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093747 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00043: (2026x) Creating use cases

- page_id: `298093747`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093747/2026x+Creating+use+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:44.497+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Use cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A use case definition specifies the required behavior of its subject relative to one or more external actors. The objective of the use case is to provide an observable result of value to one or more of its actors. The subject and the actors interact in order to achieve this objective. A use case usage is a usage of a use case definition.

##### Creating use case usage and definition elements

###### Creating use case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create use case usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *use case def*for a use case definition.
  2. use case for a use case usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases' space='UAF2P'] in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

usecasedef'Provide Transportation'{ // use case definition 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actordriver:Person; 
actorenvironment:Environment; 
}

use case 'drive vehicle' :'Provide Transportation'; // use case usage

###### Creating use case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create use case usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. use case def to create a use case definition element.
  2. use case to create a use case usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Creating use case usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create use case usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click one of the following:
  1. The **use case** def button to create a use case definition element.
  2. The **use case** button to create a use case usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **use case**or the **use case def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the use case symbol.
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and objectives for use cases' space='UAF2P'] .

###### Auto-creating use case definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create use case definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Auto-creating use case usage elements via the Extract Usage command

To auto-create use case usage elements via the Extract Usage command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094190 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: (2026x) Creating View Creation dialog

- page_id: `298094190`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094190/2026x+Creating+View+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-30T18:37:54.553+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model customization > (2026x) Custom creation dialogs > (2026x) Custom View Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom View Creation dialog and submenu creation.

#### NOTE: Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Reload the project for changes made to the customized View Creation dialog/submenu to take effect. Then, specify the active View Dialog via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'].

The default name for the View Creation dialog's invocation command is**Create View**. If you create a dialog from scratch and do not [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'], the default name remains.

##### Creating a custom View Creation dialog/submenu

To create a custom View Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the ViewCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']
5. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomViewCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{// redefining and renaming the *ViewCreationDialog* 
} 
}

##### Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

###### Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, declare a part usage, name it (optional), then subset the itemCategories element.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{partcustomSysMLViewsCategory:>itemCategories; // adding a command category} 
}

###### Specifying the view creation command category attributes for the View Creation dialog/submenu

#### INFO: Command category attributes

Command category attributes

View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined in the *3DS SysML Customization* library's *DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Components::ItemsCategory* and *AbstractItem*are:

- attribute :>> label default "Symbolic Views" ; // attribute specifies the category name
- attribute:>> isCollapseddefaulttrue;// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default

- If the dialog/submenu contains only one command category, then the category name is not displayed. In this case, both the *label* and *isCollapsed* attributes are ignored.
- If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the *isCollapsed* attribute is ignored.

To specify the view creation command category attributes for the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
2. Update the values as needed.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{partcustomSysMLViewsCategory:>itemCategories {attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue; // specifying the command category's default collapse state 
attribute:>>labeldefault"SysML v2 Symbolic Views"; // specifying the command category's name}}}

##### Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu commands

You can customize the View Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which view creation commands it should contain. The view creation commands themselves are created by targeting view templates (e.g., *generalViewTemplate*, etc.). There can be two types of templates:

- predefined templates . These templates are available via the DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates , which contains predefined templates for all standard predefined view definitions ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbolic views' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space='UAF2P'] ).
- custom templates . You need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] for any custom-created view definitions (e.g., [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'] ) if you want their corresponding view creation commands to be available in the View Creation dialog/submenu.

###### Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem and subset abstractItems . This element instantiates the new command.
2. Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the label attribute and specifying the command's name.
3. Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate and redefine operation. In the element's body, specify the in direction based on the view template you want to use for the command using a metaclassification expression. Alternatively, you can also use *MetadataAccessExpression*, e.g., when the templated view is named.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{ 
partcustomSysMLViewsCategory:>itemCategories{

// view creation command by targeting a custom view template:partreqViewItem:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new view creation command instance 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Custom Requirements View";// specifying the command's name 
performaction: DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{// specifying the view template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
in ref= (customRequirementsViewTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement;// the specified template is a custom view template, see the procedure *'Creating view templates for custom templated commands'* 
} 
}

// view creation command by targeting a predefined view template: 
partgvButton: DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new view creation command instance 
attribute:>>labeldefault"General View";// specifying the command's name 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{// specifying the view template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
in ref= (DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates::generalViewTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement;// the specified template is a predefined view template from *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates* 
} 
}

} 
} 
}

###### Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands

To create view templates for custom templated view creation commands

1. Within the body of the new dialog instance's package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] for the custom view template.
2. Within the package, declare a view usage defined by the custom view definition.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
packagecustomRequirementsViewTemplate{ // the new template's package, which you will have to target using the*OperationFromTemplate*to create a view creation command for the custom element 
view:CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View'; 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;"><span>See the following procedures for custom View Creation dialog and submenu creation.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b09d7af-ded0-486b-aae5-058cd288ea7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Reload the project for changes made to the customized View Creation dialog/submenu to take effect. Then, specify the active View Dialog via the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activating View Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dc4e467-89a8-453a-9988-427ffa5f8437"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The default name for the View Creation dialog's invocation command is </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Create View</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span> If you create a dialog from scratch and do not <ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming the Create View command"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[set a new name for the command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the default name remains.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating a custom View Creation dialog/submenu</h3><p>To create a custom View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c194eaa3-576c-4685-9fb0-7f3dc1a5c9f0">In the Containment tree,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Declaring a root namespace"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new root namespace]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="eaf8ef83-3b57-4fcb-929c-c8e2fb07e610"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>for the new namespace.</li><li data-uuid="782ee7ad-8e78-4dd2-bbf7-10d8088ce72a">In the Textual Editor,<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a package element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>and name it.</li><li data-uuid="ca4db45c-0bd9-4b25-8e4b-dc019e4c709b">Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the <em>ViewCreationDialog</em> definition from the <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs </em>package, then, within the body of the definition:<ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /></ac:link> </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu commands"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage the View Creation dialog/submenu commands]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /></ac:link></span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="4c9871a0-b912-4d37-bfb9-41815cbeef4f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcc6ce71-0e7d-4eef-b211-eec5504ca6fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomViewCreationDialog</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::ViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// redefining and renaming the <em>ViewCreationDialog</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h3><h4>Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p>To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="990416e5-7d29-44d7-9bb4-3e8dee95be18">Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, declare a part usage, name it (optional), then subset the <em>itemCategories</em> element.</li><li data-uuid="97a3d61b-9184-4477-b609-98144fa3c8e0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3c51360-ac52-422f-9f08-5d03243f6bd0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customSysMLViewsCategory </span></u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt; </span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><u>itemCategories</u>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// adding a command category</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying the view creation command category attributes for the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="465cdc0f-0ac8-4e22-8466-37a2e5702ba3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command category attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined in the <em>3DS SysML Customization</em> library's <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Components::ItemsCategory</em> and <em>AbstractItem</em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> are:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93159f0d-5d9b-4f21-bc29-df0c3b3ee045"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="dfed924b-7bb8-42a3-b018-d27f6e54419e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Symbolic Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the category name<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="53bbefd7-d7de-4154-a503-955fdb19652b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span>// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91143ee5-8abf-4dac-961d-5c247ae8e45b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="d44609ea-5f68-46c2-8141-d4eec2075db6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains only one command category, then the category name is not displayed. In this case, both the <em>label</em> and <em>isCollapsed</em> attributes are ignored.</span></li><li data-uuid="456c3fd0-3162-4e01-be4d-3b540817f8e0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the <em>isCollapsed</em> attribute is ignored.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To specify the view creation command category attributes for the View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="99f927c6-d301-4fa5-a3f0-da74bdde0f3b">Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="a6024812-efb6-4cac-934b-bbf3296b1dc1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Update the values as needed.</span></li><li data-uuid="ed3cd92c-ba5a-474e-bbe9-2bc755a1ba36"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82af2bdf-5927-4b6c-bf2f-b60511904329"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewCustomViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customSysMLViewsCategory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">         <u>attribute</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the command category's default collapse state</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;SysML v2 Symbolic Views&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the command category's name</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><p>You can customize the View Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which view creation commands it should contain. The view creation commands themselves are created by targeting view templates (e.g., <em>generalViewTemplate</em>, etc.). There can be two types of templates:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f48c3bf8-d50e-4497-af35-feb8c537ebde"><strong>predefined templates</strong>. These templates are available via the <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates</em>, which contains predefined templates for all standard predefined view definitions (<ac:link ac:anchor="Symbolic views"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbolic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tabular]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). </li><li data-uuid="38502b66-9b46-4a90-b94a-5e229e0d1f56"><strong>custom templates</strong>. You need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create custom view templates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /></ac:link> for any custom-created view definitions (e.g., <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom symbolic view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) if you want their corresponding view creation commands to be available in the View Creation dialog/submenu.</li></ul><h4>Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e1a153c2-36de-441e-bb64-6475df3cf7b1">Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem</em> and subset <em>abstractItems</em>. This element instantiates the new command.</li><li data-uuid="3a596508-f535-4935-b526-9fbe872a96ab">Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the <em>label </em>attribute and specifying the command's name.</li><li data-uuid="69506dff-f552-48fc-8bf8-329724ebe451">Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate</em> and redefine <em>operation. </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the element's body, specify the </span><em>in</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> direction </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">based on the view template you want to use for the command using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f9cb8149-6279-469c-b31c-58060532684a">metaclassification expression</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ca0c5fa-5676-455f-b7a1-716295f8e7b9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Alternatively, you can also use <em>MetadataAccessExpression</em>, e.g., when the templated view is named.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="ec9c9f24-cda3-4d7f-b72c-e5ba16797a36"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d99a8172-fc34-448c-b498-1aa6696c6e9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customSysMLViewsCategory</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">itemCategories</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">            <u>// view creation command by targeting a custom view template:</u><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">reqViewItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DialogItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">abstractItems</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view creation command instance</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Custom Requirements View&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the command's name</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span specifying the view template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                    </span><span is a predefined view template from <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands </h4><p>To create view templates for custom templated view creation commands </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="df35a4ac-822e-469c-bcf1-310e2a9e296e">Within the body of the new dialog instance's package, <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a package element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the custom view template.</li><li data-uuid="470ab27b-c7c7-4eec-b87c-5f53ee3d47b0">Within the package, declare a view usage defined by the custom view definition.</li><li data-uuid="6a2feee6-9bee-4981-85df-c3df91277af2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6552abf0-5716-48ec-b12c-41d20ff5fc0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the new template's package, which you will have to target using the<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">OperationFromTemplate</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span> </span>to create a view creation command for the custom element</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRequirementsView::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

## PAGE 00045: (2026x) Creating views

- page_id: `298094011`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094011/2026x+Creating+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:33:24.785+01:00`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Procedures for creating views depend in the view kind. See the table below and choose the appropriate page.

| View rendering kind | Documentation |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Graphical views | Symbolic views |  |
| Custom symbolic views |  |  |
| Tabular views | Tabular views |  |
| Custom tabular views |  |  |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093709 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: (2026x) Cross features

- page_id: `298093709`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093709/2026x+Cross+features
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:29:29.542+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Connection
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A cross feature allows you to linkthe value at one end of the connection with the value at the other end.It imposes a semantic constraint ensuring that the connected elements are related via a defined reference path in the model.Cross features can be specified for ends of certain connectors, such as[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Connection' space='UAF2P']or[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interface' space='UAF2P'].

Cross features can be specified for connector definition or usage ends. There are two options for specifying them:

1. Declaring cross features in the related types of the connector definition (using cross subsetting).
2. Declaring cross features in connector ends (owned cross features), options:
  1. Notation:
    1. Full notation
    2. Shorthand notation (only for connector usages)
  2. Named or unnamed

Cross feature specification includes cross multiplicity, which constrains the number of connections, rather than end values. Unlike regular multiplicity, cross multiplicity is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the *end*elements of a connector. For end elements, cross multiplicity is specified after the *end* keyword, followed by the ordering and/or uniqueness for at least one of the ends.

Please note that cross features is the correct mechanism to specify multiplicities of the connection definitions and usages while connection end multiplicities are fixed to [1] in SysML v2.0. The multiplicities that were specified on the association ends in SysML v1.x models have to be specified on cross features and not on the connection ends when porting models to SysML v2.0.

##### Specifying cross features for connector ends

###### Specifying cross features for connector ends via the Textual Editor

Specifying cross features for connector ends via the Textual Editor

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Declare cross features in the related types of the connector usage/definition:
    1. Specify cross multiplicity and ordering for the related types.
    2. Specify the connector usage's/definition's end features to subset cross features owned by the related type. Cross subsettingIdentify the usage element that you want to be the subsetting one.Follow the subsetting element with either one of the following:keyword*crosses*textual symbol*=>*Follow with a feature chain in which the first feature is the otherconnection end and the second feature is the cross feature for that end.
  2. Declare cross features for ends:
    1. Full notation:
      1. (Optional) Specify the cross feature name.
      2. Specify the kind keyword for the ends.
      3. Specify cross multiplicity, ordering, and/or uniqueness on at least one of the ends. Optionally, specify a cross feature name after the end keyword.
    2. Shorthand notation (available only for connector usages):
      1. Declare the *connection***keyword, specify the element name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space='UAF2P'] (optional) . State the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features prefixed with cross multiplicity. With shorthand notation, cross features in ends do not accept names; ends do not accept ordering.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

50%

/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */

/* cross features owned by the end type (uses cross subsetting); Step 1.a */

partdefHub{ 
refpartconnectedDevices[1..*]ordered:Device; 
} 
partdefDevice{ 
refpartconnectingHub[0..1] :Hub; 
} 
connectiondefDeviceConnection{ 
/* unnamed cross features in ends */ 
endparthub:Hub=>device.connectingHub; // the kind keyword *part*is optional 
endpartdevice:Device=>hub.connectedDevices; 
}

/* ------------ connection usage ------------ */

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*]; 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnection{

/* cross features owned by the end type (uses cross subsetting) */ 
endhub::>networkHubs=>device.connectingHub; 
enddevice::>networkDevices=>hub.connectedDevices;}}

50%

/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */

/* cross features owned by the ends; Step 1.b */

partdefHub; 
partdefDevice;

// named cross features in ends:

connectiondefDeviceConnection{endcrossHub[0..1]parthub:Hub; // the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
endcrossDevice[1..*]orderedpartdevice:Device; 
}

// unnamed cross features in ends:

connection def DeviceConnection { 
 end [0..1] part hub : Hub;// the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
 end [1..*] ordered part device : Device; 
}

/* ------------ connection usage ------------ */

// named cross features owned by ends (full notation):

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*]; 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnection{

/* cross features owned by ends; cross features named in ends (full notation) */ 
endcrossHub[1]parthub::>networkHubs; // the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
endcrossDevice [0..4]orderedpartdevice::>networkDevices; 
}}

// unnamed cross features owned by ends (full notation):

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*]; 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnection{

/* cross features owned by ends; cross features unnamed in ends (full notation) */ 
end[1]parthub::>networkHubs; // the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
end[0..4]orderedpartdevice::>networkDevices;

}}

// cross features owned by ends (shorthands):

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*];

/* cross features owned by ends (shorthands) */ 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnectionconnect[1]hub::>networkHubsto[0..4]device::>networkDevices; 
// OR (equivalent to the above) 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnectionconnect([1]hub::>networkHubs,[0..4]device::>networkDevices); 
}

##### Moving cross features between end types and ends

Although there are two options for specifying cross features for ends, you can easily refactor connector definition ends from one option to the other.

To move cross features between end types and ends

- Right-click the end of a connector definition and in the shortcut menu, select Refactor , then click one of the following:
  - **Move Cross Feature to End** to move cross features declared in the related types of the connector definition to the connector ends.
  - Move Cross Feature to End Type to move cross features declared in the connector ends to the related types of the connector definition. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

State the features explicitly (using the<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">connect...to<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">syntax</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">)<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">or in a comma-separated list of related features prefixed with cross multiplicity. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f3e2a47-0a2f-4b13-ad3e-b03881282ef5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>With shorthand notation, cross features in ends do not accept names; ends do not accept ordering. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e4a0fe80-3731-4bf0-99f9-f5a022fc0429">Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60c400dd-afd3-4e1c-9556-bf9ffb111d6e"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03aa8d19-3a35-43d9-bbf0-996378c45fb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afeb8adf-22f8-4c0c-a462-70e736fa61e1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* cross features owned by the end type (uses cross subsetting); Step 1.a */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">connectedDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">..*] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">connectingHub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* unnamed cross features in ends */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> =&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">connectingHub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the kind keyword <em>part </em>is optional </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> =&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">connectedDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* ------------ connection usage ------------ */</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NetworkConfiguration</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkConnections</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* cross features owned by the end type (uses cross subsetting) */</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> =&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">connectingHub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> =&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">connectedDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1"
