ac:macro-id="61d67a40-6283-4b55-a5d7-98e89cf9841f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* cross features owned by the ends; Step 1.b */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named cross features in ends:</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">crossHub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the kind keyword (<em>part) </em>is mandatory</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">crossDevice </span>[</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">..*] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed cross features in ends:</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span> {<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the kind keyword (<em>part) </em>is mandatory</span></span></span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span>..*] <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span>;<br />}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* ------------ connection usage ------------ */</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named cross features owned by ends (full notation):</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NetworkConfiguration</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkConnections</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">/* cross features owned by ends; cross features named in ends (full notation) */<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">crossHub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the kind keyword (<em>part) </em>is mandatory</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">crossDevice</span> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed cross features owned by ends (full notation)</span>:</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NetworkConfiguration</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [*];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkConnections</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* cross features owned by ends; cross features unnamed in ends (full notation) */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the kind keyword (<em>part) </em>is mandatory</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ordered</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// cross features owned by ends (shorthands): </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NetworkConfiguration</span><span cross features owned by ends (shorthands) */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span OR (equivalent to the above)</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkConnections</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Moving cross features between end types and ends</span></h3><p>Although there are two options for specifying cross features for ends, you can easily refactor connector definition ends from one option to the other. </p><p>To move cross features between end types and ends</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="1fa72273-702f-43c9-8ea8-b848414e0114">Right-click the end of a connector definition and in the shortcut menu, select<span> </span><strong>Refactor</strong>, then<span> click one of the following:</span><ul><li><span><strong>Move Cross Feature to End</strong> to move cross features declared in the related types of the connector definition to the connector ends.</span></li><li><strong>Move Cross Feature to End Type </strong>to move cross features declared in the connector ends to <span>the related types of the connector definition.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cross_features_refactoring.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Cross features" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094180 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: (2026x) Custom creation dialogs

- page_id: `298094180`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094180/2026x+Custom+creation+dialogs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-02-05T10:00:41.763+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are several creation dialogs and submenus available in the modeling tool:

- Element Creation dialog and/or submenu are available via the Create Element command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific elements.
- Relationship Creation dialog and/or submenu are available via the Create Relationship command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific relationships.
- View Creation dialog and/or submenu are available via the Create View command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific views.
  - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] page.

The corresponding commands are available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element.

Instead of using predefined creation dialogs that come with the modeling tool, you can create custom model-based creation dialogs listing only the commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the creation dialogs based on the needs of your specific project.

Currently, only the **View Creation dialog**is customizable in the modeling tool. Customizations for element and relationship creation dialogs will be available in future versions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Relationship Creation submenu, Element Creation dialog, and View Creation submenu.

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The*3DS**SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesUIComponents***namespace that enables the customization of creation dialogs:

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094112 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00048: (2026x) Custom style sheets

- page_id: `298094112`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094112/2026x+Custom+style+sheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-02-05T10:00:10.841+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules.

##### Working with style sheets

Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. There can be several types of style sheets:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Predefined style sheets' space='UAF2P'] . The predefined style sheets are located in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package of the*3DS**SysML Customization*library. There are two predefined style sheets in the*DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package:
  - DefaultStyleSheet
  - BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] . There are two types of style sheets you can create:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] This option allows you to create your own custom rules as well as reuse and customize all the rules already available in the reused style sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., *DefaultStyleSheet*) or a custom-created one. For example, you can reuse the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet, then customize its rules by changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the DefaultStyle*Sheet* 's rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the DefaultStyle*Sheet* .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] This option allows you to create your own custom rules only without reusing any predefined or custom style sheets and their rules . Choose this option if you prefer creating your own rules without the need to reuse or customize the existing ones.

##### Style sheet composition

A style sheet is comprised of the following components:

- Rules - specify conditions and styles for symbols.
  - Condition - specifies to which symbols the rule applies.
  - Style - specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition.

#### PANEL: Example of a rule

Example of a rule

partattributeRule :> rule { 
part:>> condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { // the 'condition' component specifying a predefined condition *ByElementMetaclassCondition* that the rule applies to symbols 
refitem:>> elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.metadata); // specifying the condition with applicable element metaclasses 
} 
view:>> style : AbstractDeepPurpleStyle; // the 'style' component specifying a predefined style *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle* found in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*package 
}

- Style definitions . There are several types of style definitions :
  - **Primary style definitions** - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles* of the *3DS SysML Customization*library.
    - Primary style definitions cannot be edited and should be used to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
    - For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'].
  - **Auxiliary style definitions** - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:
    - Predefined style definitions - style definitions specified in the DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions package of the *3DS SysML Customization*library (e.g., AbstractGreenStyle , AbstractRedStyle , etc.).
      - Predefined style definitions cannot be edited, but they can be customized locally to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
      - For a list of predefined style definitions, see the 'Predefined style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'].
    - **Custom style definitions** - style definitions you create yourself.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

// primary style definitions *SymbolStyle*and *ShapeStyle*in the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles*package

viewdefSymbolStyle { 
attributelineWidth : Integerdefault1; 
attributefillColor : Colordefault"#FFF0F0F0"; 
attributeuseFillColor : Booleandefaulttrue; 
attributepenColor : Colordefault"#FF424242"; 
attributetextColor : Colordefault"#000000"; 
attributefont : Fontdefault"Roboto,0,11"; 
attributewrap : Booleandefaultfalse; 
}

viewdefShapeStyle:>SymbolStyle{ 
attributecontentFillColor:Colordefault"#FFFFFFFF"{ 
} 
attributetextVerticalPosition:TextVerticalPositionEnumdefaulttextVerticalPosition::Top{ 
} 
attributecompartmentFillColor:Colordefault"#FFF0F0F0"; 
attributecompartmentTextColor:Colordefault"#000000"; 
}

// predefined (auxiliary) style definition *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle*specified for the *attributeRule* rule (external specification) in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*package

part attributeRule :> rule { 
 part:>> condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { 
 ref item :>> elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.metadata); 
 } 
 view:>> style : AbstractDeepPurpleStyle; // the predefined style definition *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle***is specified for the style component of the *attributeRule* rule (external specification) 
}

viewdefAbstractDeepPurpleStyle:>ShapeStyle{ // predefined (auxiliary) style definition *AbstractDeepPurpleStyle*in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*packagethat subclassifies *ShapeStyle* 
attribute:>>fillColordefault"#E6DEF3"; 
attribute:>>compartmentFillColordefault"#F8F5FB"; 
attribute:>>penColordefault"#20125F"; 
}

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesStyles*namespace, dedicated to symbol styles' customization:

- <DS_Styles> DassaultSystemesStyles
  - CoreStylesComponents
    - Contains style sheet modeling and configuration concepts.
    - **KerMLStyles**. Contains primary style definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet rules . For a list of the primary style definitions, their ref items, and their compatibility with element symbols, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.
    - **SysMLStyles**. Contains compartment style view definitions for style sheet rules.
    - **Predicates**. Contains condition part definitions for style sheet rules. To learn more about reusing and modifying existing or creating custom rules using primary style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] pages.
  - **StyleSheets**. Contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Predefined style sheets' space='UAF2P']. You can reuse and customize the predefined style sheets or create new ones from scratch. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P'] page.
    - **ColorDefinitions**. Contains color definitions for style sheet rules. You can reuse and customize the predefined style definitions. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] page.For a list of predefined style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.
    - **CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions**. Contains compartment visibility definitions for style sheet rules .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules. </span></p><h3>Working with style sheets</h3><p>Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">There can be several types of style sheets:</span></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Predefined style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The predefined style sheets are located in the <em style="text-align: left;">DS_Styles::StyleSheets</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>package of the<span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>3DS </em></span><em style="text-align: left;">SysML Customization</em></span><span> </span>library.</span></span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">There are two predefined style sheets in the<span> <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets</em> </span>package:</span></span><ul><li><em>DefaultStyleSheet</em></li><li><em>BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet</em></li></ul></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[User-created style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. There are two types of style sheets you can create:<ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Reusing an existing style sheet"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Reuse an existing style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">This option allows you to create your own custom rules as well as reuse and customize all the rules already available in the reused </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">style sheet. The reused style sheet can be either a predefined one (e.g., <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em>) or a custom-created one. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For example, you can reuse the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet, then customize its </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">rules </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">by changing their names, attributes, and their values, etc. Additionally, if you create rules only for selected symbols, overwriting the<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">DefaultStyle<em>Sheet</em></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">'s rules, all the other symbols you do not overwrite will continue to be represented according to the </span><em style="text-align: left;">DefaultStyle<em>Sheet</em></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a custom style sheet from scratch"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a custom style sheet from scratch]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />This option allows you to create your own custom rules only without reusing any predefined or custom style sheets and their rules<span>. </span>Choose this option if you prefer creating your own rules without the need to reuse or customize the existing ones. </li></ul></li></ul><h3>Style sheet composition</h3><p>A style sheet is comprised of the following components:</p><ul><li><strong>Rules </strong>- specify conditions and styles for symbols.<strong> </strong><ul><li><strong>Condition </strong>- specifies to which symbols the rule applies.</li><li><strong>Style </strong>- specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition. </li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddf2c046-984a-4c9a-8497-3e9a511dc208"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of a rule</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> attributeRule :&gt; rule {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the 'condition' component specifying a predefined condition <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em> that the rule applies to symbols</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">); <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the condition with applicable element metaclasses</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; style : AbstractDeepPurpleStyle; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the 'style' component specifying a predefined style <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</em> found in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><strong>Style definitions</strong>. There are several types of style definitions<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">:</span><ul><li><span><strong>Primary style definitions</strong> - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the <em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles</em> of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library. </span><ul><li><span>Primary style definitions cannot be edited and should be used to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subclassify a custom style definition (external specification)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[define the style of a specific rule (local specification)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /></span></li><li><span>For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span><strong>Auxiliary style definitions</strong> - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:</span><ul><li><strong>Predefined style definitions</strong> - style definitions specified in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions</em> <span>package of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library </span>(e.g., <em>AbstractGreenStyle</em>, <em>AbstractRedStyle</em>, etc.).<ul><li><span>Predefined style definitions cannot be edited, but they can be customized locally to <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[define the style of a specific rule (local specification)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span>For a list of predefined style definitions, see the 'Predefined style definitions' column in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span><strong>Custom style definitions</strong> - style definitions you create yourself.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e80881c-27ec-4397-9ddb-43357bfd6363"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Explanation in textual notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// primary style definitions <em>SymbolStyle </em>and <em>ShapeStyle </em>in the <em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles </em>package</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <u>SymbolStyle</u> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> lineWidth : Integer </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> fillColor : Color </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FFF0F0F0&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> useFillColor : Boolean </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> penColor : Color </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FF424242&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> textColor : Color </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#000000&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> font : Font </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Roboto,0,11&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> wrap : Boolean </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SymbolStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">contentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FFFFFFFF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textVerticalPosition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TextVerticalPositionEnum</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textVerticalPosition::Top</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FFF0F0F0&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentTextColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Color</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#000000&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// predefined (auxiliary) style definition <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specified for the <em>attributeRule</em> rule (external specification) in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package</span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> attributeRule :&gt; rule {<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; condition : ByElementMetaclassCondition { </span><br />      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; elementMetaclass = (SysML::Systems::AttributeUsage.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>, SysML::Systems::AttributeDefinition.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">);</span><br />   }<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; style : <u>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</u>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// t<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">he predefined style definition <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em> </em></span>is specified for the style component of the <em>attributeRule</em> rule (external specification)</span></span> </span></span><br />}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AbstractDeepPurpleStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// predefined (auxiliary) style definition <em>AbstractDeepPurpleStyle </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions </em>package </span>that subclassifies <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>ShapeStyle</em></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#E6DEF3&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">compartmentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#F8F5FB&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">penColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#20125F&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library contains the <em>DassaultSystemesStyles </em>namespace, dedicated to symbol styles' customization:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="fbd212af-3ed1-4274-a314-94652a3ca2c4"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;DS_Styles&gt; DassaultSystemesStyles</span></strong><ul><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CoreStylesComponents</span></strong><ul><li>Contains style sheet modeling and configuration<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">concepts.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>KerMLStyles</strong>.<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains primary style</span> definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3034b41d-fff4-465a-ac38-35c0abc7bbb1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a list of the primary style definitions, their ref items, and their compatibility with element symbols, see /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customizing style sheet components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094018 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00049: (2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions

- page_id: `298094018`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094018/2026x+Custom+symbolic+view+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-02-05T10:01:56.363+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating symbolic views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined symbolic view definitions, you can create model-based custom symbolic view definitions as part of your projects.By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic views with customized palettes.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of a custom symbolic view definition *Requirements View*and a view *Stakeholder Needs*defined by the custom-created view definition*.*

##### Custom symbolic view definition composition

Custom symbolic view definitions differ in the view palettes they may have. A view palette contains button categories, which contain category-specific buttons. Button categories may also contain menu buttons with individual buttons in their submenus. The button categories also have specific attributes specifying the category name, tooltip, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. 
The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.

Creating custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes is enabled by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'], which contains all the necessary elements and configurations, allowing you to create your own symbolic view definitions with customized view palettes.

The library also contains the *Base Symbolic View*definition which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom symbolic view definitions. Itcarries the predefined view palette via the *baseViewPalette*part, containing Selection, Tools, and Common button categories. It also carries the rendering feature that indicates how the elements should be displayed in the view.See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'].

##### General button categories table

The table below lists the buttons and attributes used in the general button categories - Selection, Tools, Common - used in all predefined symbolic view definitions. These button categories are also used in the *<bsv> 'Base Symbolic View'*via its *baseViewPalette.*You can find the button categories and predefined view palettes in *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*.

| Button categories | Category buttons | Category attributes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SelectionCategory | selectionButtoncompleteCoverageModeButtonselectionMenu:selectSiblingButtonselectOfTheSameTypeButtonselectConnectedButtonselectConnectedRecursivelyButtonselectAllButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default false; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default false; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Selections"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Selection"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |
| ToolsCategory | stickyButtonpusherButtonmagnetButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default false; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default false; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Tools"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Tools"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |
| CommonCategory | textBoxMenu:textBoxButtontextBoxHtmlButtonseparatorMenu:horizontalSeparatorButtonverticalSeparatorButtonrectangularSeparatorButtondocButtoncommentButtonimageButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default true; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default true; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Common"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Common"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The *3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesUIComponents*namespace that enables the custom symbolic view definition creation:

INLINE

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- **<DS_Functions> DassaultSystemesFunctions**. Contains various functions used for customization elements.
- <DS_Styles> DassaultSystemesStyles
  - CoreStylesComponents
    - Contains style sheet modeling and configuration concepts.
    - **KerMLStyles**. Contains primary style definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet rules .
    - **SysMLStyles**. Contains compartment style view definitions for style sheet rules.
    - **Predicates**. Contains condition part definitions for style sheet rules.
  - **StyleSheets**. Contains predefined style sheets.
    - **ColorDefinitions**. Contains color definitions for style sheet rules.
    - **CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions**. Contains compartment visibility definitions for style sheet rules .
- <DS_UIComponents> DassaultSystemesUIComponents
  - CoreUIComponents
    - **Operations**. Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.
    - **Components**. Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.
    - **Palette**. Contains concepts designed for view palette creation.
    - **Dialogs**. Contains concepts designed for Creation dialog customization.
  - **UIComponents**.
    - Contains a predefined view creation dialog.
    - **ViewsTemplates**. Contains model elements used to define the actions of templated buttons for creation dialogs, which in turn duplicate the template into the model.
- **<DS_Views> DassaultSystemesViews**
  - **CoreViews**. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.
  - **SymbolicViews**. Contains predefined symbolic views.
  - **SymbolicViewsByExpression**. Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.
  - **TabularViews**. Contains predefined tabular views.
  - **ViewPalettes**. Contains predefined view palettes .
    - **CodeActionIdentifiers**. Contains action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view palettes . The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation.
    - **ElementTemplates**. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.

data-uuid="722ef772-74d9-45ed-8022-1bfe3eb851b9"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;DS_Styles&gt; DassaultSystemesStyles</span></strong><ul><li data-uuid="4f13b3ff-8506-45f1-91bc-f16f6ecb7e05"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">CoreStylesComponents</span></strong><ul><li data-uuid="e4975c04-f483-418e-9aa4-ea510035b006">Contains style sheet modeling and configuration <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">concepts.</span></li><li data-uuid="641c4b2f-0543-4348-960c-9959455b8108"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>KerMLStyles</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains primary style</span> definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span></li><li data-uuid="c835c0fb-cadb-4e5c-b3bc-39b52503c6a3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SysMLStyles</strong>. </span>Contains compartment style view definitions for style sheet <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules.</span></li><li data-uuid="2fee7b5e-6b3b-4f68-af57-68b0e602a5c0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Predicates</strong>. </span>Contains condition part definitions for style sheet <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules.</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="a2bad032-446d-4446-901c-0632fab97776"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>StyleSheets</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined style </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sheets.</span><ul><li data-uuid="34d4b916-f2cc-4129-b008-b36390de3f84"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ColorDefinitions</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains color definitions </span>for style sheet <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules.</span></li><li data-uuid="e862fd09-da62-4c58-b005-1afc4afefce5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions</strong>. </span>Contains compartment visibility definitions for style sheet <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rules</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">&lt;DS_UIComponents&gt; DassaultSystemesUIComponents</span></strong><ul><li data-uuid="e042d711-9c2c-4d38-bb8d-410d8f667549"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">CoreUIComponents</span></strong><ul><li data-uuid="9ffaf5b7-d928-493c-aada-3114eda412d2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Operations</strong>. </span>Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.</li><li data-uuid="922916a1-8aaf-438f-805c-0ace6225f355"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Components</strong>. </span>Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.</li><li data-uuid="f7242004-e0b2-48cf-b85d-2af3ab30fdf8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Palette</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains concepts designed </span>for view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> creation.</span></li><li data-uuid="e8d471f1-3617-47ba-9716-a5c2e689973c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Dialogs</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains concepts designed </span>for Creation dialog customization.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="d26313a9-373d-4bef-b8c6-b3e3b9159527"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>UIComponents</strong>. </span><ul><li data-uuid="fb05d3b7-b90e-4b7f-9820-22a241b570af">Contains a predefined view creation dialog.</li><li data-uuid="bc032e70-868f-42dc-9001-54c66da0a7f6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ViewsTemplates</strong>. </span>Contains model elements used to define the actions of templated buttons for creation dialogs, which in turn duplicate the template into the model.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>&lt;DS_Views&gt; DassaultSystemesViews</strong></span><br /><ul><li data-uuid="9282fc4d-0bd1-46cd-8b92-8d0444754a2e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CoreViews</strong>. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.</span></li><li data-uuid="a8313e07-84ce-48e4-9348-94bfa8f9fd85"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined symbolic views.</span></li><li data-uuid="aef80962-78a3-4c60-823a-7b3d6b73c594"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViewsByExpression</strong>. </span>Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.</li><li data-uuid="dfa2207e-9d5f-4c9a-a0ec-fd68b98c8159"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>TabularViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined tabular views.</span></li><li data-uuid="44d33d30-c97e-4056-8be3-c46f320cbf27"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ViewPalettes</strong>. </span>Contains predefined view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><ul><li data-uuid="1948ff62-ab94-40d3-8e79-1b7cf6bca27b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CodeActionIdentifiers</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains </span>action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span>. The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation. </li><li data-uuid="df86da1b-08fb-4921-b5fd-1b71c37bb2c8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ElementTemplates</strong>. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094038 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00050: (2026x) Custom tabular view definitions

- page_id: `298094038`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094038/2026x+Custom+tabular+view+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-02-05T10:02:56.512+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Creating views > (2026x) Creating tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined tabular view definitions, you can create model-based custom tabular view definitions as part of your projects.By defining custom view definitions, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P'] with customized columns.

##### Custom tabular view definition composition

Custom tabular view definitions differ in the columns they may have. While the tool comes with predefined tabular view definitions, you can model custom tabular view definitions with tailored columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of (1) predefined tabular view *Generic Table*, (2) customized *Generic Table* with the *Owned Element* columns removed, (3) custom *Drone Variant Table* with custom columns.

There are two ways you can create custom tabular view definitions:

- Reuse predefined tabular view definitions
  - Reusing a predefined tabular view definition allows the new view definition to inherit the columns defined for the reused definition. You can modify the inherited columns and add new custom ones, too.
- Create new customized tabular view definitions from scratch
  - A new view definition does not inherit any columns, allowing you to specify all the columns yourself.

Creating custom tabular view definitions with customized columns is enabled by the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P'],which contains all the necessary elements and configurations allowing you to create your own tabular view definitions withcustomized columns.The library also contains the *Base Table*viewdefinition, which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom tabular view definitions. The library also carries predefined tabular view definitions with predefined columns.See the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tabular view definitions' space='UAF2P'].

##### Predefined tabular views' columns table

The table below lists all the columns used in the predefined tabular view definitions.

| Standard predefined tabular view definition | Columns |
| --- | --- |
| Generic table | Declared NameDeclared Short NameDocumentationQualified NameOwnerOwned Element |
| Requirements table | Declared NameReq IDDocumentation |

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The*3DS SysML Customization*library contains**the*DassaultSystemesViews*namespace that enables the custom tabular view definition creation:

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom symbolic view definitions' space='UAF2P']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094183 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00051: (2026x) Custom View Creation dialog

- page_id: `298094183`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094183/2026x+Custom+View+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:55:06.196+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model customization > (2026x) Custom creation dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The View Creation dialog and/or submenuare available via the**Create View**command.This command is available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element.The dialog and the submenu display a list of commands for creating specific views. Additionally, all commands can be structured into named categories.

While there is a predefined submenu and dialog for displaying the predefined commands, you can either customize the predefined dialog or create a new one from scratch. This way, you cancreate a custom model-based View Creation dialog listing only the view creation commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the View Creation dialog based on the needs of your specific project.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The image on the left shows the View Creation submenu according to the predefined*SysMLViewCreationDialog*configuration. The image on the right shows the customized View Creation submenu.

##### View Creation dialog composition and customization

The Create View command can invoke either a View Creation dialog or the command's submenu. The submenu is displayed when there are no more than ten view creation commands. Otherwise, the View Creation dialog is displayed instead of the submenu. Both the dialog and the submenu display the command categories containing the view creation commands.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The image displays the View Creation dialog (predefined) and the View Creation submenu (custom) with commands structured into categories.

There are two options for customizing the View Creation dialog/submenu:

- Customizing the predefined SysMLViewCreationDialog configuration for the View Creation dialog/submenu. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] page.
- Creating the View Creation dialog/submenu from scratch via the conceptual *ViewCreationDialog*definition. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] page.

The view dialog/submenu, command categories, and individual commands have specific attributes available for customization. Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents* package with the *SysMLViewCreationDialog* dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.

You can activate the needed View Creation dialog via the **Active Create View Dialog**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents</em> package with the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.</span></p><p><span>You can activate the needed View Creation dialog via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Active Create View Dialog</strong>. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing View Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> page.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094119 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00052: (2026x) Customizing style sheet components

- page_id: `298094119`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094119/2026x+Customizing+style+sheet+components
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:37:23.912+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Symbol styles > (2026x) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for customizing the style sheet components.

- The procedures on this page use the example of customizing rules of the DefaultStyleSheet style sheet, however, customization of any other style sheet's rules, whether predefined or custom, is analogous.
- For procedures on supplementing customized style sheets with your own custom rules (instead of or in addition to customizing existing ones), see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] page.

##### Customizing style sheet components

###### Reusing style sheet**rules

To reuse style sheet rules

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space='UAF2P']Reuse a**style sheet (e.g., DefaultStyleSheet ).
2. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, declare a part usage, specify a new name for the rule (optional), redefine the needed rule, and in its body, do any of the following as needed:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
  3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. partPortRule rule defined in the DefaultStyleSheet style sheetpackageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
 privateimportPredicates::*; 
 privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
 privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{partcustomizedPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ // redefining and renaming the *partPortRule* rule 
// customize the redefined rule 
} 
} 
}

###### Modifying the style definition defining the style of a rule

To modify the style definition defining the style of a rule

1. Reuse a style sheet**rule[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
2. Declare a view usage, define it by the needed auxiliary style definition, and redefine the inherited part *style*. You can define the view by multiple style definitions, listing them in a comma-separated list.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
partcustomizedPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ 
view:AbstractCyanStyle:>>style; // the *AbstractGreenRule*inherited from the *partPortRule*via the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet is replaced with the predefined style definition *AbstractCyanStyle* 
} 
}

###### Modifying the styleattributes locallyfor the style of a rule

There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:

- Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] .
- External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for the style specification of different rules. See the subsection [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheet components' space='UAF2P'] page.

To modify the style attributes locally for the style of a rule

1. Reuse a style sheet**rule[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
2. Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part *style*, and in its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value. The attributes available to the specified style definition depend on the style definitions they subclassify. For an extensive list of the predefined style definitions and the primary style definitions they subclassify, and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. part def NewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
partcustomizedPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ 
view:>>style{ 
attribute :>> fillColor default "#D9EEDA"; // redefining the attribute *fillColor* available via the predefined style definition *AbstractGreenRule* 
} 
} 
}

###### Modifying thecompartment styleof a rule

The majority of the compartments are disabled (set to *false*) for most of the elements in the *DefaultStyleSheet*style sheet via the *HideAllCompartments* predefined compartment style definition.

To modify the compartment style of a rule

1. Reuse a style sheet**rule[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing style sheet components' space='UAF2P'].
2. Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part *style*. In its body, declare a part for the needed compartment and define it by its definition. In its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value. To determine which primary compartment style definitions (including their attributes and value types) are available for each element symbol, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='UAF2P'] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='UAF2P']. partdefNewStyleSheet:>DefaultStyleSheet{ 
partnewPartPortRule:>>partPortRule{ 
view:>>style{ 
partattributeCompartment:AttributesCompartmentStyle{ 
:>>showCompartmentdefaulttrue;// the inherited *attributeCompartment* part's *showCompartment* attribute's value is changed to *true* 
} 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
