style </span><span>of a rule</span></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c238023d-21db-494d-b79b-b6ad468a9051"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The majority of the compartments are disabled (set to <em>false</em>) for most of the elements in the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet via the <em>HideAllCompartments</em> predefined compartment style definition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify the compartment style of a rule </p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Reusing style sheet rules"><ac:link-body>Reuse a style sheet<em> </em>rule</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing style sheet components" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part <em>style</em>. In its body, declare a part for the needed compartment and define it by its definition. In its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value.</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8c8880-e28e-45bb-9747-60e585fc5aca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which primary compartment style definitions (including their attributes and value types) are available for each element symbol, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol compartment styles table"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol compartment styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b872d7c9-2193-4f1f-b8bb-a09d36e490e0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DefaultStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newPartPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">attributeCompartment </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AttributesCompartmentStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the inherited <em>attributeCompartment</em> part's <em>showCompartment</em> attribute's value is changed to <em>true</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><br /></span></span></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094187 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00053: (2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog

- page_id: `298094187`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094187/2026x+Customizing+View+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-30T10:32:22.067+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model customization > (2026x) Custom creation dialogs > (2026x) Custom View Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for customizing the predefined *SysMLViewCreationDialog* configuration for the View Creation dialog and submenu.

The procedures on this page use the example of customizing the*SysMLViewCreationDialog***part definition. However, you can similarly customize any other custom view creation dialog customization part definition you may have.

#### NOTE: Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Reload the project for changes made to the customized View Creation dialog/submenu to take effect. Then, specify the active View Dialog via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'].

##### Reusing a View Creation dialog/submenu

To reuse a View Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space='UAF2P'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Textual Editor' space='UAF2P'] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space='UAF2P'] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the SysMLViewCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::UIComponents package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']
  3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P']
5. Click the Synchronize button.

package CustomViewCreationDialogs { 
 part def CustomizedViewCreationDialog :>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ // redefining and renaming the *SysMLViewCreationDialog*} 
}

##### Renaming the Create View command

The default name for the View Creation dialog's invocation command is **Create View**.

To rename the Create View command

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the actionName attribute with the new view creation command name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
attribute:>> actionNamedefault"Create Diagram";// renaming the view creation command to "Create Diagram"}

##### Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

#### INFO: Command categories

Command categories

View Creation dialog/submenu command categories predefined in the *3DS SysML Customization* library's *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog*are:

- sysMLViewsMenu - for the symbolic views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu.
- sysMLTableMenu - for the tabular views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu.

###### Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Removing view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu

To remove view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the SysMLViewCreationDialog definition and set the multiplicity to zero.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLTableMenu[0];// removing the *sysMLTableMenu* command category by setting the multiplicity to zero 
}

###### Modifying the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu

#### INFO: Command category attributes

Command category attributes

View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for *sysMLViewsMenu*and *sysMLTableMenu*in *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog*are:

- attribute :>> label default "Symbolic Views" ; // attribute specifies the category name
- attribute:>> isCollapseddefaulttrue;// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default

- If the dialog/submenu contains only one command category, then the category name is not displayed. In this case, both the *label* and *isCollapsed* attributes are ignored.
- If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the *isCollapsed* attribute is ignored.

To modify the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the SysMLViewCreationDialog definition.
2. Within the body of the redefining category, redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
3. Update the values as needed.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLViewsMenu{ 
attribute:>> isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified command category default collapse state 
attribute:>> labeldefault"SysML v2 Symbolic Views";// modified command category name 
} 
}

##### Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu commands

#### INFO: Commands in categories

Commands in categories

View Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for *sysMLViewsMenu*and *sysMLTableMenu*in *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog*are:

- sysMLViewsMenu:
  - gvItem - command for General View
  - ivItem - command for Interconnection View
  - afvItem - command for Action Flow View
  - stvItem - command for State Transition View
- sysMLTableMenu:
  - gtItem - command for General Table
  - rtItem - command for Requirements Table

###### Removing view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To remove view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category*.*
2. Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
 part:>>sysMLViewsMenu{ 
part:>>afvItem[0]; // removing the *afvItem* command from the *sysMLViewsMenu*category by setting the multiplicity to zero 
} 
}

###### Renaming view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To rename view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category and declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to modify.
2. Within the body of the redefining usage, redefine the inherited *label*attribute with the needed value.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLViewsMenu{ 
part:>>afvItem{ 
attribute:>> labeldefault"Action Flow Diagram"; // modified command name 
}} 
}

###### Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">sysMLTableMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>]</u>; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the <em>sysMLTableMenu</em> command category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Modifying the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e30ba43-c8da-497e-9daf-45a436f217ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command category attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2725aea4-a54a-4e36-9039-d8ab57ed21d1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f39f2d1d-3b98-49cf-bc24-19055cfd44b0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Symbolic Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the category name<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="1b7d057b-3ac9-41cb-9e13-a0d0c1722f9d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span>// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f372b4-2387-4654-8e9e-76748d2ef534"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4cce9b81-4155-4f89-b96f-fcbbef889c68"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains only one command category, then the category name is not displayed. In this case, both the <em>label</em> and <em>isCollapsed</em> attributes are ignored.</span></li><li data-uuid="7ac17c98-5d7e-4715-9e45-627033e07fdd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the <em>isCollapsed</em> attribute is ignored.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c1a3070f-5c6b-4aa3-a030-defdf6a6ef6a">Within the body of the part definition element of the new  View Creation instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition.</li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining category, </span>redefine <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span></li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Update the values as needed.</span></li><li data-uuid="37ec15cb-4691-49d7-8d63-b1d8478f84ef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d85b0fb-cb16-4ef0-8d6a-b5ead1b9832f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="465518da-8e26-400a-b5b8-7fd47b90ee9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Commands in categories</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5e50ad2a-7b75-49d7-87e7-a3e3ee5b4270"><em>sysMLViewsMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gvItem - </em>command for General View</li><li><em>ivItem - </em>command for Interconnection Table</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Removing view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To remove view creation commands style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.</span></li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093581 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: (2026x) Dependency

- page_id: `298093581`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093581/2026x+Dependency
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:13:55.382+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A dependency is a kind of relationship between any number of client (source) and supplier (target) elements. This implies that a change to a supplier element may result in a change to a client element.Dependencies can be useful for representing relationships between elements in an abstract way.Dependencies can be binary, with one client and supplier, or n-ary, with one client and more than one supplier.The n-ary dependency is displayed as a dot symbol with segments consisting of dependency paths and client/supplier symbols.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *Use*dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source) and*Data Layer*is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source). The packages*Data Layer*and*External Interface Layer*are the supplier (target).The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

Dependency is deleted if client or supplier count in the dependency is reduced zero.

##### Creating dependencies

###### Creating dependencies via the Textual Editor

To create dependencies via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the dependency keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the client (source) element. Then follow with the keyword to and the name(s) of the supplier (target) element(s) listed in a comma-seperated list. If the dependency has no name, the keyword*from*can be omitted.
3. (Optional) Specify a body with any annotating elements owned by the dependency via annotation relationships.
4. Click the Synchronize button. package'Data Layer';package'Service Layer';package'External Interface Layer';dependencyfrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // unnamed dependencydependencyUsefrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // named dependencydependency from 'Service Layer' to 'External Interface Layer', 'Data Layer'; // unnamed n-ary dependency (with more than one supplier (target))dependency'Service Layer'to'Data Layer'{ // unnamed dependency with a body 
/* 'Service Layer' is the dependency client, 'Data Layer' is the supplier. */ 
}

###### Creating dependencies via the view palette

To create dependencies via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **dependency** button.
2. In the view pane, click on the client (source) and then on the supplier (target) to create a dependency path between them.

###### Creating dependenciesvia the Create Relationship command

To create dependencies via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click dependency . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Creating n-ary dependencies via the view

To create n-ary dependencies via the view

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the client command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's client. Repeat for all the needed elements.
  2. Click the supplier command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's supplier. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary dependencies

###### Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the view

To convert binary dependency to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary dependency you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary dependency is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-arydependencytobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary dependency to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the dependency you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary dependency dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has one client and supplier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A dependency is a kind of relationship between any number of client (source) and supplier (target) elements. This implies that a change to a supplier element may result in a change to a client element. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Dependencies can be useful for representing relationships between elements in an abstract way. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Dependencies can be binary, with one client and supplier, or n-ary, with one client and more than one supplier.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> The n-ary dependency is displayed as a dot symbol with segments consisting of dependency paths and client/supplier symbols.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c717842c-923b-4492-874d-e8c14f6648ab"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fbe55327-7008-4062-a3f7-b8122bee34da"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays a <em>Use </em>dependency where the package <em>Service Layer </em><span>is the client (source) and </span><em>Data Layer </em><span>is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ccbb03c-d0f8-45df-b6dd-25c32262dd5f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nary_dependencies.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package <em>Service Layer </em><span>is the client (source). The packages </span><em>Data Layer </em><span>and </span><em>External Interface Layer </em><span>are the supplier (target). </span><span>The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b8e67ab-543e-45ba-9840-2b63c6faf5e8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dependency is deleted if client or supplier count in the dependency is reduced zero.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating dependencies</h3><h4>Creating dependencies via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create dependencies via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">dependency </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword. </span></li><li>Specify the element name (optional),<span> </span>then the <em>from<span> </span></em>keyword and the name of the client (source) element. Then follow with the keyword<span> </span><em>to<span> </span></em>and the name(s) of the supplier (target) element(s) listed in a comma-seperated list. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5664809-1834-4f0b-b3c6-0c79277afc95"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the dependency has no name, the keyword<span> </span><em>from<span> </span></em>can be omitted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>(Optional) Specify a body with any annotating elements owned by the dependency via annotation relationships.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de1dc05f-b34f-4963-b560-25ddf828339d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Data Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Service Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'External Interface Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Service Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'External Interface Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed dependency</span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Service Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'External Interface Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named dependency</span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Service Layer'</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'External Interface Layer'</span>, <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Data Layer'</span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed n-ary dependency (with more than one supplier (target))</span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Service Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Data Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed dependency with a body</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* 'Service Layer' is the dependency client, 'Data Layer' is the supplier. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating dependencies via the view palette</h4><p>To create dependencies via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the<span> <strong>dependency</strong></span><strong> </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>In the view pane, click on the client (source) and then on the supplier (target) to create a dependency path between them.</li></ol><h4>Creating <span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">dependencies </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p>To create dependencies via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click on the client (source) element.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>dependency</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Creating n-ary dependencies via the view</h4><p>To create n-ary dependencies via the view</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the diagram"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Convert a binary dependency to a n-ary]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /></ac:link>. </li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:</span><ol><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>client<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's client. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>supplier<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's supplier. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary dependencies</h3><h4>Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary dependency to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary dependency you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary dependency is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">n-ary </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">dependency </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">to </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">binary </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary dependency to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the dependency you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary dependency dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the dependency has one client and supplier. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093645 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: (2026x) Derive requirement

- page_id: `298093645`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093645/2026x+Derive+requirement
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:26:20.667+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Derived requirements are created using the derive relationship via the derivation connection. The derive relationship links more detailed requirements to their original, higher-level requirements. A derivation connection asserts that one or more derived requirements are derived from a single original requirement. The single end for the original requirement should subset the original requirement, while the rest of the ends should subset derived requirements. Once the original requirement is satisfied, the derived ones must also be satisfied.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

Derivation is deleted if original or derived requirement count in the derivation is reduced zero.

##### Deriving requirements

###### Deriving requirements via the Textual Editor

To derive requirements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the derivation and import the RequirementDerivation library.
2. Declare the #derivation conncetion keyword, specify the derivation name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional).
3. In the derivation body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the derived requirement. Repeat for multiple derived requirements.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in separate packages

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling; 
} 
packageDerivations{// derivations package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
privateimportRequirements::*;// requirements package import 
#derivationconnection : 'Derived System Requirements'{// unnamed specialized (feature typing) binary derivation connection 
end#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement end 
end#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement end 
} 
}

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in the same package

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling;#derivationconnection'heating & cooling derivations'{// named n-ary derivation connectionend#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement endend#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement endend#derive::>'cooling';// derived requirement end}}

###### Deriving requirements via the view palette

To derive requirements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:
  1. The **derivation** button to create a derivation connection.
    1. In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.
  2. The **n-ary****derivation** button to create an n-ary derivation connection.
    1. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.
    2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **original** end command.
    3. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.
    4. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **derived** end command.
    5. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation**,**n-ary derivation**,****or the**derivation****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view

To create additional n-ary derivations via the view

1. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the original end command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement.
  2. Click the **derived end** command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary derivations

###### Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view

To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryderivationtobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier.

##### Creating derivation connection definition elements

###### Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the #derivation connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] of the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] of the derived requirement.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. #derivationconnectiondef'Derived system requirements'{ 
end#originaloriginalReq:vehicleRequirements; 
end#derivederivedReq:engineRequirements; 
}

###### Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='UAF2P']page.

###### Creating derivation definitions via the view palette

To create derivation definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **derivation** **def** button to create a derivation definition element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation** or**n-ary derivation**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    } <br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deriving requirements via the view palette</h4><p>To derive requirements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:<br /><ol><li>The<span> <strong>derivation </strong></span>button to create a derivation connection.<ol><li>In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.</li></ol></li><li>The<span> <strong>n-ary </strong><strong>derivation</strong></span><span> </span>button to create an n-ary derivation connection.<ol><li>Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.</li><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the<span> <strong>original </strong></span><strong>end<span> </span></strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.</li><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the<span> <strong>derived </strong></span><strong>end<span> </span></strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="208c68ea-9cec-45e6-a15b-dd18b8d3f1f3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <span><strong>derivation</strong>,<strong> n-ary derivation</strong>,<strong> </strong></span>or the<span> <strong>derivation </strong></span><strong>def<span> </span></strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view</h4><p>To create additional n-ary derivations via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>original end<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement. </li><li>Click the<span> <strong>derived end</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary derivations</h3><h4>Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span>n-ary </span><span>derivation </span><span>to </span><span>binary </span><span>via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating derivation connection definition elements</h3><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>#derivation connection def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the <em>end</em> <em>#original </em>keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the original requirement. </li><li>Specify the <em>end</em> <em>#derive </em>keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the derived requirement. </li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">#derivation </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Derived system requirements' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #original </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">originalReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #derive </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">derivedReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>derivation</strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>def </strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">button to create a derivation definition element.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35819f6b-9a74-40cd-bfc8-eac27c667eb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>derivation</strong> or<strong> n-ary derivation </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093813 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00056: (2026x) Directed features for actions

- page_id: `298093813`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093813/2026x+Directed+features+for+actions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:57:20.312+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actions may have features with directions in, out or inout that are considered to be parameters of the action. Features with direction in or inout are input parameters, and features with direction out or inout are output parameters. An action usage inherits the parameters of its definitions, if any, and it can also define its own parameters or redefine those of its definitions. Directed features may also be bound to indicate the equivalence of their values.

##### Specifying directed features for actions

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the action's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name. actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; // *in* directed feature 
outpicture:Picture; // *out* directed feature}

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the view palette

To specify directed features for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the action for which you want to create the element.

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an action symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

##### Binding directed features

Directed features can also bebound. An output parameter of one action usage may be bound to the input parameter of another action usage. Such a binding indicates that the values of the target input parameter will always be the same as the values of the source output parameter. For more information on binding, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space='UAF2P'] page.

actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; 
outpicture:Picture;

// binding *in*parameters TakePicture.scene with focus.scene 
bindscene=focus.scene; 
 
actionfocus:Focus{inscene;outimage; } 
firstfocusthenshoot; 
flowfocus.imagetoshoot.image; 
actionshoot:Shoot{inimage;outpicture; } 
 
// binding *out*parameters TakePicture.picture with shoot.picture 
bindpicture=shoot.picture; 
}

The binding of action parameters does not model the case when there is an actual transfer of items between actions. Transfers can be modeled using flows, succession flows as well as send and accept actions. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Transfers between actions' space='UAF2P'] page.

ac:macro-id="41a88ea9-c03d-468d-a567-5aedf51a687e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The binding of action parameters does not model the case when there is an actual transfer of items between actions. Transfers can be modeled using flows, succession flows as well as send and accept actions. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Transfers between actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Transfers between actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093910 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00057: (2026x) Directed features for states

- page_id: `298093910`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093910/2026x+Directed+features+for+states
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:23:00.764+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specify features with directions *in*, *out,*or *inout*for states that act as the parameters of the state. Directed features can also be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space='UAF2P'].

##### Specifying directed features for states

###### Specifying directed features for states via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for states via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the state's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name.

statedefVehicleStates{inoperatingVehicle:Vehicle;}

###### Specifying directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select a state symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

###### Specifying directed features for states via the view palette

To specify directed features for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the state for which you want to create the element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094068 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00058: (2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views

- page_id: `298094068`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094068/2026x+Displaying+elements+in+symbolic+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:50:04.693+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Display commands to display element symbols in the graphical representation of a symbolic view.

##### Display commands

| Display command category and its commands | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Nested-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the nested-view . Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as nested symbols. |
| Display Features | The command displays owned and inherited features of any metatype. |
| Display Features Recursively | The command displays all owned and inherited features of any metatype recursively. |
| Display Parts | The command displays owned and inherited parts. |
| Display Parts Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited parts recursively. |
| Display Actions | The command displays owned and inherited actions. |
| Display Actions Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited actions recursively. |
| Display Swimlanes | The command displays owned and inherited swimlane symbols, and any element symbols related to each swimlane.The command is only available for specific and . |
| Display Owned Members | The command displays owned members based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. The Display Others In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.The command is only available for . |
| Display Owned Members Recursively | The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. The Display Others In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.The command is only available for . |
| Tree-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view .Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.Contains all the same commands as the Nested-view style category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.The Display Features command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style. |
| Relationships | The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths of the selected symbol. |
| Display Connectors | The command displays all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. Connector paths can also be . |
| Display Specializations | The command displays all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. |
| Display Others | The command displays other relationships (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. |
| In-Between Relationships | The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths only between the selected element symbols.Contains all the same commands as the Relationships category.This category is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view. |
| Other | The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols. |
| Display Ports | The command displays owned and inherited ports.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent. |
| Display Parameters | The command displays owned and inherited directional features.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent. |
| Display Ends | The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.The command is only available for . |
| Display Exposed Elements | The command displays elements exposed for the view. Exposed elements depend on the (e.g., recursivity) and . The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a .This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an . |

#### NOTE: Recursive commands

Recursive commands

Recursive commands are indicated by the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the *drone in SAR mission*part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.

##### Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)Displaying element symbols in a view

There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the Display commands to be available (except for the Display Exposed Elements command).

###### Displaying element symbols

To display element symbols

1. In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display. To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.Use the **Select**buttons in the **Select**option group in the view panel.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Click the Display button [ATTACHMENT filename='display_button.png'] in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.
  2. Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select Display . Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.

##### Displaying connectors in a view

In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol using the**Display Connectors**command. See the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='UAF2P']procedure.

###### Proxy connectors

Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via feature chain.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partlugBoltJoints:LugBoltJoint[0..5]; 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
partmountingHoles:LugBoltMountingHole[5]; 
} 
} 
connect[0..1]lugBoltJointsto[1]wheel.w.mountingHoles; 
}

###### In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part *lugBoltJoints*and part *mountingHoles* nested within part *w*, which is nested within the part *wheel*. 
Since the nested part symbols*w*and*mountingHoles*are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between*lugBoltJoints*and*wheel*, which is a displayed parent element symbol of*w*and*mountingHoles.*To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part*wheel*and the connector path.A connector label with the*w.mountingHoles*feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to.

###### Elaborating a connector

The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.

To elaborate/simplify a connector

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Elaborate [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is elaborated.
  2. To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Simplify [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying n-ary connectors in a view

In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.

The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) N-ary connection' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) N-ary interface' space='UAF2P']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Derive requirement' space='UAF2P']

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency' space='UAF2P'] can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths for the n-ary dot symbol via the **Display Others**command.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

###### Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view

To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view

- Select the containing element symbol and click the Display command, then in its dropdown menu click Display Features . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view

To display n-ary connector ends in a view

- Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the Display command. In its dropdown menu, click Display Ends . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

- Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the Display command, then Display Connectors in the Relationships category. [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
