style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">   Display Owned Members Recursively</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. </span></span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Display Others</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c94082d4-7e6b-409e-9d3d-da42cc1774fd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command is only available for <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Tree-view style</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><span>The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">O</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">wned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.</span></p><ul title=""><li data-uuid="6c51ea38-75c0-4f4f-98de-b673abe64f18"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains all the same commands as the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Nested-view style </strong>category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="a943e155-e603-435d-8ab2-29b9ef148836"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The <strong>Display Features</strong> command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Relationships</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths of the selected symbol.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Connectors</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Connector paths can also be </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Elaborating a connector"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Specializations</td><td><span>The command displays all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view</span>.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Others</td><td><span>The command displays other relationships (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.</span></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>In-Between Relationships</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><span>The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths only between the selected element symbols.</span></p><ul title=""><li data-uuid="33ab12ff-5f17-4e76-b389-d5a7f8643c77"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Contains all the same commands as the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Relationships</strong> category.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="f5f2bab8-0bda-4461-8ff3-b19bd11fcac7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">This category is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><strong title="">Other</strong></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ports</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited ports.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea0381f5-1e71-42b5-8879-4601ea095343"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Parameters </td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited directional features.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71db2b8d-7fa0-4306-8ff7-8971ca47c54b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ends</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18987458-ba47-4c34-847d-ee44388c3570"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command is only available for <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying n-ary connectors in a view"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[n-ary dot symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span>   Display Exposed Elements</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays elements exposed for the view. <span>Exposed elements depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposition kind]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., recursivity) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7866e435-7592-4369-ade6-4bcb219fed2e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4acb477e-c9ee-4f2b-9122-83b716c28268"><span>The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="18dd3bff-bbf5-408d-a8d2-3c366eeee6eb"><p>This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display exposed elements notification.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f9bdb1c-48d3-4406-b1a5-fc676442b857"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Recursive commands</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Recursive commands are indicated by the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_cmd.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_commands.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the <em>drone in SAR mission </em>part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.</h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd72916b-65f7-44c1-ba2b-3b9ed1c54c30"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying element symbols in a view</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722af941-19b0-4426-a909-ef6eb0ae1b9b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the Display commands to be available (except for the Display Exposed Elements command).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Displaying element symbols</span></h4><p>To display element symbols</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c2dfbd-2cfc-428a-b545-87f6715e5c90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f41dad1-5ad0-45d3-a18e-743b57ba085f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.</li><li>Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol. </li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.</li><li>Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.</li><li>Use the <strong>Select </strong>buttons in the <strong>Select </strong>option group in the view panel.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Do one of the following:</span></p><ol><li>Click the <strong>Display</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.</li><li>Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong>. Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Displaying connectors in a view</h3><p>In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Connectors </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command. S</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ee the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying element symbols"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> procedure.</span></p><h4>Proxy connectors</h4><p>Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via feature chain.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b62cb38-d1ca-4efd-af82-be2fb80f2bda"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4d6bd1e-49db-493e-8901-bc61ef25e8c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Proxy_connectors.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67c97922-2190-4b14-89bb-ff9834d45c70"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="544bb35c-59cb-48bf-978e-4d57ff4349f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltJoint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltMountingHole</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span>] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part <em>lugBoltJoints </em>and part <em>mountingHoles</em> nested within part <em>w</em>, which is nested within the part <em>wheel</em>.<br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Since the nested part symbols </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">lugBoltJoints </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, which is a displayed parent element symbol of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles.<br /></em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and the connector path.<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A connector label with the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w.mountingHoles</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to. </span></h6><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Elaborating a connector</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.</span></p><p>To elaborate/simplify a connector</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Elaborate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="elaborate_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>command. The connector is elaborated.</li><li>To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Simplify <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simplify_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.</li></ol></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Elaborating_a_connector.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Displaying n-ary connectors in a view</h3><p>In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.</span></p><p>The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) N-ary connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) N-ary interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Derive requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derivation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13f43002-a869-49a2-b0fa-21b855d3bb07"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths for the n-ary dot symbol via the <strong>Display Others </strong>command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e6bbe1f-1458-4bc1-9725-027fceba27c9"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d233dd2-1b3c-4eb4-80f0-62615daa7a7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binary_derivation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and </span><em>heating </em><span>is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2098e62f-9f19-4b4c-b8a1-ca39d95bd91f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_derivation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and <em>heating </em>is the derived one. </span><span>The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the containing element symbol and click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then in its dropdown menu click <strong>Display Features</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connector ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the <strong>Display</strong> command. In its dropdown menu, click <strong>Display Ends</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then <strong>Display Connectors</strong> in the<span> </span><strong>Relationships<span> </span></strong>category.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298094096 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: (2026x) Displaying elements in tabular views

- page_id: `298094096`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298094096/2026x+Displaying+elements+in+tabular+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-01-15T18:38:35.224+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) View > (2026x) Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Display elements in diagrammatic representations of tabular views.

##### Displaying elements in tabular views

To display elements in tabular views

1. Ensure the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space='UAF2P'] view has:
  1. Tabular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space='UAF2P'] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space='UAF2P'] .
  3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space='UAF2P'] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening views' space='UAF2P'] the view. The exposed and filtered elements are displayed in the tabular view.
3. (Optional) To reflect any changes in the model, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_table_button.png'] Refresh button in the view toolbar to update the view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Display elements in diagrammatic representations of tabular views.</p><h3>Displaying elements in tabular views</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To display elements in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Ensure the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view has:<ol><li>Tabular <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the view. The exposed and filtered elements are displayed in the tabular view.</li><li>(Optional) To reflect any changes in the model, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_table_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements in tabular views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the view toolbar to update the view.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093270 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00061: (2026x) Docked symbols

- page_id: `298093270`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093270/2026x+Docked+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:11:38.850+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View kinds > (2026x) Graphical views > (2026x) Symbolic views > (2026x) Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2, an element symbol can be docked to a view, acting as a context and a kind of frame for a view.

**Docked symbol details:**

- The element represented as the docked symbol is the owner of elements whose symbols are contained by the docked symbol.
- Element symbols cannot be created outside the docked symbols' shape in the view pane. Creating element symbols outside the docked symbol's frame expands the frame to contain the new symbols.
- A view can have only one docked symbol.
- The docked element symbol can be undocked and vice versa. See the procedures below.
- Executing the 'Select in Containment tree' command:
  - on the docked symbol's body - selects the corresponding view.
  - on the docked symbol's header - selects the corresponding element .

##### Docking a symbol to a view

To dock a symbol to a view

- To create a view with a docked symbol:
  - In the Containment tree, right-click a usage or a definition element and in the shortcut menu, click **Create view**.
- To dock a symbol displayed in a view:
  - Select the element symbol in the view and click the **Dock** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button on the corner of the element frame. The **Dock**button is available only if the view displays one element symbol (e.g., the docking option is not available if there are two part usage symbols displayed in the view).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Docking a symbol to a view

##### Undocking a symbol from a view

To undock a symbol from a view

- Right-click the header of the docked symbol and in the shortcut menu, select **Refactor**, then **Undock**. Right-clicking anywhere but the header of the docked symbol will not display the **Undock**command in the shortcut menu.Once you undock a symbol, it remains the same size, taking up the entire view pane. Click the **Make Preferred Size** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to shrink the symbol, allowing for more space in the view pane.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Undocking a symbol from a view

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093410 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00062: (2026x) Drag and Drop handler

- page_id: `298093410`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093410/2026x+Drag+and+Drop+handler
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:03:36.476+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements > (2026x) Managing elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands.

##### Using the Drag and Drop handler

To use the Drag and Drop handler

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.
  2. In a view, select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the Drag and Drop handler [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_and_drop_handler.png'] . Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element ownership change' space='UAF2P']. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol.
2. Drag and drop the element/symbol on:
  1. The target element symbol in a view.
  2. The appropriate field in the Specification panel. You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element ownership change' space='UAF2P'].You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.
3. The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.

##### Drag and Drop handler commands

Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols.

| Command name | Source | Target |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set TypeIf an element already has a set type, performing the Set Type command again replaces the set type. To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. | Any definition element | Any usage element |
| Set Payload | Any definition element | Flow Connection |
| Set Performed Action | Action | Perform Action |
| Set Use Case Included | Use Case | Include Use Case |
| Create Perform Action | Action, Action def | Part, Action, State, Part def, Action def, State def |
| Create Exhibit State | State, State def | Part, State, Action, Part def, State def, Action def |
| Create Include Use Case | Use Case, Use Case def | Part, Use Case, Part def, Use Case def |
| Create Objective / Create Require Constraint of Objective | Constraint, Constraint def | Case, Case def |
| Create Actor | Part, Part def | Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def |
| Create Stakeholder | Part, Part def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Frame Concern | Concern, Concern def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Require Constraint | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Assume Constraint | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Assert Constraint | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Satisfy Requirement | Requirement, Requirement def | Part |
| Create Subject | Any usage element,Any definition element | Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def |
| Apply Metadata | Metadata def | Any element |
| Create Usage | Any definition element | Any element |
| Create Subsetting Usage | Any usage element | Any element |
| Set Referenced Feature | Constraint | Assume, Require, Assert constraint |
| Concern | Frame concern |  |
| Requirement | Satisfy requirement |  |
| Set Subsetted Feature | Any usage element | Any usage element |
| Set Redefined Feature | Any usage element | Any usage element |
| Set Value | Any feature | Any feature |
| Set Superclassifier | Any definition element | Any definition element |
| Create Symbol | Any element | Any element |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands. </p><h3>Using the Drag and Drop handler</h3><p>To use the Drag and Drop handler</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="47025575-d93b-455c-98d9-595c7250a966">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.</li><li>In a view, select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the <strong>Drag and Drop</strong> handler <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_and_drop_handler.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ad23bda-3eee-4c7a-a876-604aa6d7d064"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[changes the element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The latter modifies the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.</li><li>For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying additional properties via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="52d2f91d-e275-4852-bc82-585ac7237ade">The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.</li></ol><h3>Drag and Drop handler commands</h3><p>Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Command name</th><th scope="col">Source</th><th scope="col">Target</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set Type</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69ef6f62-0f40-4d4b-9c97-e73cdc85e5f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an element already has a set type, performing the <strong>Set Type</strong> command again replaces the set type. <br />To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Any usage element</td></tr><tr><td>Set Payload</td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Flow Connection</td></tr><tr><td>Set Performed Action</td><td>Action</td><td>Perform Action</td></tr><tr><td>Set Use Case Included</td><td>Use Case</td><td>Include Use Case</td></tr><tr><td>Create Perform Action</td><td>Action, Action def</td><td>Part, Action, State, Part def, Action def, State def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Exhibit State</td><td>State, State def</td><td>Part, State, Action, Part def, State def, Action def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Include Use Case</td><td>Use Case, Use Case def</td><td>Part, Use Case, Part def, Use Case def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Objective / <br />Create Require Constraint of Objective</td><td>Constraint, Constraint def</td><td>Case, Case def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Actor</td><td>Part, Part def</td><td>Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Stakeholder</td><td>Part, Part def</td><td>Requirement, <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092872 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00063: (2026x) Editor Tabs

- page_id: `298092872`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092872/2026x+Editor+Tabs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T14:33:03.920+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment > (2026x) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Editor tabs allow you to access views, textual editor, and model samples.

##### Editor tab types

The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:

- Welcome tab. The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.
- Samples tab. It allows you to display the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.
- View Editor tab. It allows you to display and work with view. You can have multiple view tabs open simultaneously.
- Textual Editor tab. It allows you to display the model in textual notation view and further develop the model using textual notation.
- Specification tab. It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements.

##### Editor tabs commands

Editor tabs' commands can be found:

- In the Main Menu , click Window , then Editor Tabs . In the dropdown menu, view and select the appropriate command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Editor tabs allow you to access views, textual editor, and model samples.</p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c54f6dbc-5c61-4b6c-a314-2ad6f152913a">Editor tab types</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:</p><ul><li>Welcome tab.<br />The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.</li><li>Samples tab.<br />It allows you to display the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.</li><li>View Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display and work with view. You can have multiple view tabs open simultaneously.</li><li>Textual Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display the model in textual notation view and further develop the model using textual notation.</li><li data-uuid="975212db-3869-4ac2-9eec-3ae21f774437">Specification tab.<br />It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements.  </li></ul><h3>Editor tabs commands</h3><p>Editor tabs' commands can be found:</p><ul><li>In the <strong>Main Menu</strong>, click <strong>Window</strong>, then <strong>Editor Tabs</strong>. In the dropdown menu, view and select the appropriate command.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093416 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00065: (2026x) Element names

- page_id: `298093416`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093416/2026x+Element+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-22T09:52:46.908+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the information on this page for naming elements.

For information on name resolution (i.e., feature chaining and qualified names), see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] page.

- Declared name
  - In addition to the keyword indicating element kind, the declaration of an element may also specify a declared name. The declared name should be fully descriptive, particularly in the context of the element definition. Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] page. part'LED Flashlight' { // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 partbulb; // basic element name without whitespace 
}

- Declared short name
  - The declared short name, if given, should be an abbreviated name useful for referring to the element. The declared short name is distinguished by being surrounded by the delimiting characters < and >. The declared short name, if used, must precede the declared name. part <cyl> cylinders; // a part usage with the declared short name <cyl> and declared name cylindersrequirement<'1'>fullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement; // a requirement with the declared short name <'1'>, which is also considered its requirement ID.

- Name and short name
  - If neither a declared name or a declared short name are given in the declaration of a usage, then it is determined by the name or short name of the redefined usage. Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. part def Engine { 
 part <cyl> cylinders : Cylinder [2 ..*]; 
} 
part def FourCylinderEngine :> Engine { 
 part redefines cylinders [4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its name is "cylinders". 
// OR 
 part redefines cyl[4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is "cyl". Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. 
}

##### Specifying the declared name

###### Specifying the declared name via the Textual Editor

To specify the declared name via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword or declared short name with the needed basic or unrestricted name. Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space='UAF2P'] page.
2. Click the Synchronize button. part 'LED Flashlight'{ // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 part bulb; // basic element name without whitespace 
}

###### Specifying the declared name via the Specification Panel

To specify the declared name via the Specification Panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

###### Specifying the declared name via the Containment tree

To specify the declared name via the Containment tree

- In the Containment tree, double-click an element and type the needed name.

###### Specifying the declared name via the element symbol in a view

To specify the declared name via the element symbol in a view

- On a symbol on view, double-click the name line under the element keyword and type the needed name.

##### Specifying the declared short name

###### Specifying the declared short name via the Textual Editor

To specify the declared short name via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword with the <> symbols with the needed short name within them.
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
part<cyl>cylinders:Cylinder[2..*]; // a part usage with the declared short name <cyl> and declared name cylinders 
}part def FourCylinderEngine :> Engine {part redefines cyl[4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is "cyl". Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. 
}

###### Specifying the declared short name via the Specification Panel

To specify the declared short name via the Specification Panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cylinders</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Cylinder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ..*];   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a part usage with the declared short name &lt;cyl&gt; and declared name cylinders</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FourCylinderEngine</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">redefines</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cyl</span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is &quot;cyl&quot;. Note that &lt;&gt; symbols should not be used when indicating the short name.</span></span></span></span><br />}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying the declared short name via the Specification Panel</h4><p>To specify the declared short name via the Specification Panel</p><hr /><ul><li>See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093470 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00066: (2026x) Element navigation

- page_id: `298093470`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093470/2026x+Element+navigation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:48:37.768+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Go To command in the element shortcut menu to navigate from one element to its related elements based on their relationships. In the Go To submenu, the related elements are grouped into categories indicating the relationships between elements, such as Flow, Connection, Type, Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, etc.

##### Navigating elements via the Go To command

To navigate elements via the Go To command

Do one of the following:

- Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a view . In the shortcut menu, click the Go To command, and in its submenu, click the needed element.
- Select an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a view . In the main menu, click Analyze , then Go To . In the submenu, click the needed element. The element is selected in the Containment tree. If the **Go To**submenu contains more than twenty elements, it displays the **Related Elements** command that opens a submenu with the full related elements list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the *vccu*element. Clicking the *'part vccu'* element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the *vccu*part element that subsets the *vccu*element for which the command was invoked.

##### Navigating elements via the Navigation tab

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In the Go To submenu, the related elements are grouped into categories indicating the relationships between elements, such as Flow, Connection, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Type, Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, etc.</span></span></p><h3><span>Navigating elements via the Go To command</span></h3><p><span>To navigate elements via the Go To command</span></p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093433 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00067: (2026x) Element ownership change

- page_id: `298093433`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093433/2026x+Element+ownership+change
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:13:30.902+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the procedures regarding element ownership change.

##### Changing element ownership

###### Changing element ownership via the Textual Editor

To change element ownership via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element notation you want to move, including its name and the closing semicolon or the contained body. partflashlight{// to remove this part element, select it along with its name and body contained within the curly braces 
partbulb;// to remove this part element, select the entire line 
}
2. Cut the selection in one of the following ways:
  1. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+X .
  2. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click Cut .
3. Place the cursor within the curly braces of the intended owning element.
4. Paste the selection in one of the following ways:
  1. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+V .
  2. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click Paste .
5. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Changing element ownership via the Containment tree

To change element ownership via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, click the element whose owner you want to change.
2. Drag it onto the intended owner. The element is moved to the new owning element.

###### Changing element ownership via element symbol in a view

To change element ownership via element symbol in a view

1. In a view, click the symbol of the element whose owner you want to change.
2. Drag it onto the element symbol of the intended owner. The element is moved to the new owning element. Dragging and dropping the symbol itself changes the element's ownership which is different from the functionalities provided by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'].

###### Changing element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view

To change element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon.
2. Click on the element you want to be the owner, then the element you want to designate as the owned one. A feature membership path is created between the elements.

###### Changing element ownership via the view palette

To change element ownership via the view palette

1. In the View Palette, under the Other Relationships group, select feature membership .
2. Draw a line with the diamond symbol at the end of the element symbol you want to be the owner.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093902 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00068: (2026x) Entry, do, and exit actions for states 

- page_id: `298093902`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093902/2026x+Entry+do+and+exit+actions+for+states%C2%A0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:33:59.757+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you create the needed states for your model, you can further specify them as needed. Most commonly, states are specified with specially identified action usages, such as the *entry*, *do*, and *exit* actions.

##### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states

A state definition or usage can contain specially identified action usages that are only performed while the state is activated.

- An entry action starts when the state is activated.
- A do action starts after the entry action completes and continues while the state is active.
- An exit action starts when the state is exited, and the state becomes inactive once the exit action is completed.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the state's body, do one of the following:
  1. Define empty actions by declaring entry/do/exit keyword followed by a semicolon.
  2. Define actions within the state by declaring entry action/do action/exit action keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space='UAF2P'] (optional), then either end the element specification or specify the element body.
  3. Specify actions referencing those defined elsewhere in the model by declaring entry/do/exit keyword followed by the name of the action you want to reference, then either end the element specification or specify the element body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

stateon{

// -------- Defining empty actions - Step 1.a 
entry; 
do; 
exit;

// -------- Defining actions within the state - Step 1.b

entryactionperformSelfTest{invehicle=operatingVehicle;} 
doactionprovidePower{} 
exitactionapplyParkingBrake:ApplyParkingBrake; // defining and feature typing actions within the state

// -------- Specifying actions that reference actions defined elsewhere - Step 1.c

entryperformSelfTest; 
doprovidePower; 
exitapplyParkingBrake; 
}

###### Specifying entry actions for states via the view palette

To specify entry actions for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click the entry **action**button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the entry action symbol.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a state symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
  - Select a state symbol and on the right side of the actions compartment, click the Create Element button. In the displayed menu, select the needed action. The new element is created and displayed in the actions compartment.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'].
2. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu.
3. In the **actions** compartment section's bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. In the shortcut menu, select the needed action. The element is created in the **actions** compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once you create the needed states for your model, you can further specify them as needed. Most commonly, states are specified with specially identified action usages, such as the <em>entry</em>, <em>do</em>, and <em>exit</em> actions.</p><h3>Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states </h3><p>A state definition or usage can contain specially identified action usages that are only performed while the state is activated.</p><ul><li>An entry action starts when the state is /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view </p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a state symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.</li><li>Select a state symbol and on the right side of the <strong>actions</strong> compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>Element </span></strong>button. In the displayed menu, select the needed action.<strong><span> </span></strong>The new element is created and displayed in the <strong>actions</strong> compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel</h4><p>To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Entry, do, and exit actions for states " /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>actions</strong> compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Entry, do, and exit actions for states " /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button. </span>In the shortcut menu, select the needed action. <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>actions</strong> compartment.<br /></span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093506 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: (2026x) Enumeration

- page_id: `298093506`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093506/2026x+Enumeration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:49:43.166+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An enumeration definition's instances are limited to a set of enumeration usages, which are the enumerated values of the enumeration definition. An enumeration usage is defined by an enumeration definition and is restricted to only the set of enumerated values specified in the definition.

##### Creating enumeration usage and definition elements

###### Creating enumeration usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create enumeration usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *enum def*for an enumeration definition.
  2. *enum* for an enumeration usage. The declaration of an enumerated value within the body of an enumeration definition may omit the *enum* keyword.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the enumeration's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. attributedefConditionLevel{ 
attributecolor:ConditionColor; 
}enumdefConditionColor{red;green;yellow; } 
// OR 
enumdefConditionColor{ 
enumred; 
enumgreen; 
enumyellow; 
} 
 
enumdefRiskLevel:>ConditionLevel{ 
enumlow{:>>color=ConditionColor::green; } 
enummedium{:>>color=ConditionColor::yellow; } 
enumhigh{:>>color=ConditionColor::red; } 
}

###### Creating enumeration usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create enumeration usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. enum def to create an enumeration definition element.
  2. enum to create an enumeration usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the enumeration's features.

###### Creating enumeration usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space='UAF2P'] page.

To create enumeration usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an enumeration definition element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the enum element.
  - Select an enumeration symbol and on the right side of the enums compartment, click the Create enum button. The new element is created and displayed in the enums compartment.

###### Creating enumeration usage elements via the Specification panel

To create enumeration usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space='UAF2P'] of an enumeration definition.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **enum**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **enums**compartment section's bar, click the **Create enum**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **enums** compartment.

###### Creating enumeration definition elements via the view palette

To create enumeration definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **enum** def button to create an enumeration definition element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the enumeration symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the enumeration's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
