<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298092894 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: (2026x) Environment options

- page_id: `298092894`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298092894/2026x+Environment+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-10T17:47:17.664+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides information on the SysML v2-specific environment options you can use to customize your workflow.

##### Opening the Environment Options dialog

To open the **Environment Options** dialog

- From the Options menu, select Environment . Then select either SysML v2 or SysML v2 Textual Editor group.

##### Modifying environment options

To modify environment options

1. Open the **Environment Options** dialog[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Environment options' space='UAF2P'] .
2. Locate the option you want to modify. Use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick filter' space='MT']uick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']Click the **Show Description** button to display the environment option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true / false , or selecting a value from the list. Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to restore default option values.
4. Click OK .

##### SysML v2 Environment Options

| Option | Default value | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| General |  |  |  |
| Show Element Keyword | true | true/false | Set to true to display element keywords (e.g., part, action, actor) across the UI, such as in the Containment tree, Specification panel, and shortcut menu, while excluding views. |
| SysML v2 Textual Editor |  |  |  |
| Code Style |  |  |  |
| Add Blank Lines Between Textual Notation Blocks | false | true/false | Set to true to insert a blank line after each textual notation block in the textual notation to separate the blocks. |
| Display Specializations as Keywords | false | true/false | Set to true to display specializations as keywords rather than symbols in the textual notation. >]]> |
| Display Metadata as Keywords | false | true/false | Set to true to display metadata as keywords rather than symbols in the textual notation. >]]> |
| Use Short Name Instead of Name | true | true/false | Set to true to display the element's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. >]]>This option applies only when an element is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting an element in a view). If the element is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the element is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target element will be displayed. |
| Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement | false | true/false | Set to true to display the requirement's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. >]]>This option applies only when a requirement is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting a requirement in a view). If the requirement is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the requirement is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target requirement will be displayed. |
| Max Column for Hard Wrap | 170 | Number | Specify the maximum number of columns for hard wrapping lines in the Textual Editor. When a line exceeds the specified limit, it is split into sections of new indented lines to fit the line length based on the maximum number of columns. When wrapping, the editor follows specific rules, such as breaking at keywords ("connect", "from", "to", "of"), binary operators ("+", "-"), commas, and long single-line bodies. Set to -1 to disable the max column. |
| Editor |  |  |  |
| Synchronize Model with Errors | false | true/false | Set to true to synchronize the model with the textual notation, even if the notation contains errors. >]]> |
| Name Resolution Error Limit | 10 | Number | Specify the name resolution error limit to control the handling of name resolution errors. When specified, the name resolution stops if the error limit is reached, leaving the remaining names unresolved. This limit applies independently to each open Textual Editor tab. Set to -1 to disable the error limit and allow name resolution to continue for all names, skipping only those with errors. >]]> |
| Force KerML Namespaces Opening | false | true/false | Set to true to force the opening of KerML namespaces in the Textual Editor. |
| Preferred Element Name for Autocompletion | Short name | Short nameNameBoth names | Specify which element name should be suggested in context-sensitive autocompletion. If the selected name type is not specified for an element, autocompletion will suggest an existing name type instead. >]]> |
| Enable Sticky Header | true | true/false | Set to true to enable the sticky header in the Textual Editor, which displays the owner of the currently visible notation at the top of the editor. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. >]]> |
| Sticky Header Line Count | 5 | Number | Specify the maximum number of lines to display in the sticky header. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. >]]> |
| Font Size | 14 | Number | Specify the default font size. |
| Text Generation |  |  |  |
| Smart Generation Timeout | -1.0 | -1.0 - ∞ | Specify the timeout duration for smart generation in seconds. During textual notation generation, the tool generates the shortest qualified names. If the timeout is specified, a simpler name is generated once the time limit is reached. Set to -1 to disable the timeout. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page provides information on the SysML v2-specific environment options you can use to customize your workflow.</p><h3>Opening the Environment Options dialog</h3><p>To open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. Then select either <strong>SysML v2</strong> or <strong>SysML v2 Textual Editor </strong>group.</li></ul><h3>Modifying environment options</h3><p>To modify environment options</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Environment Options dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Environment options" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Locate the option you want to modify.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7672be49-876c-41ce-9a52-f49c111fe1d2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Quick filter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[q]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>uick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_quick_filter.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Environment options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click the <strong>Show Description</strong> button to display the environment option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar" ac:alt="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_group_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Environment options" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to <em>true</em>/<em>false</em>, or selecting a value from the list.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f945ab-1085-4e2c-9a06-d8bd3009f2a3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to restore default option values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>SysML v2 Environment Options</h3><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 80.7214%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.856%;" /><col style="width: 11.284%;" /><col style="width: 13.3513%;" /><col style="width: 46.5141%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Option</th><th scope="col">Default value</th><th scope="col">Value options</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><th class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" scope="colgroup" data-highlight-colour="#deebff">SysML v2 </th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">General</span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Show Element Keyword</span></td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display element keywords (e.g., <em>part</em>, <em>action</em>, <em>actor</em>) across the UI, such as in the Containment tree, Specification panel, and shortcut menu, while excluding <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">views</span>.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong title="">SysML v2 Textual Editor</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background color : " colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">Code Style</td></tr><tr><td>Add Blank Lines Between Textual Notation Blocks</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to insert a blank line after each textual notation block in the textual notation to separate the blocks.</td></tr><tr><td>Display Specializations as Keywords</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to display specializations as keywords rather than symbols in the textual notation. <ac:link ac:anchor="Keywords"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Display Metadata as Keywords</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set to true to display metadata as keywords rather than symbols in the textual notation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Metadata" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Use Short Name Instead of Name</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set to true to display the element's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Element names" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdff2ca8-2aba-4707-bf44-d9357e0e7923"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option applies only when an element is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting an element in a view). If the element is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the element is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target element will be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Use Short Name Instead of Name for Requirement</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set to true to display the requirement's short name instead of its name in the textual notation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Element names" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d32625b-caed-4639-a0fd-e4bf9deeedd4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option applies only when a requirement is targeted outside the Textual Editor (e.g., when subsetting a requirement in a view). If the requirement is directly targeted in the Textual Editor by typing its name, it will not be automatically replaced with the short name. However, if the requirement is first targeted in a view and then opened in the Textual Editor, the short name of the target requirement will be displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Max Column for Hard Wrap</td><td>170</td><td>Number</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specify the maximum number of columns for hard wrapping lines in the Textual Editor. When a line exceeds the specified limit, it is split into sections of new indented lines to fit the line length based on the maximum number of columns. When wrapping, the editor follows specific rules, such as breaking at keywords (&quot;connect&quot;, &quot;from&quot;, &quot;to&quot;, &quot;of&quot;), binary operators (&quot;+&quot;, &quot;-&quot;), commas, and long single-line bodies. Set to -1 to disable the max column.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background color : " colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">Editor</td></tr><tr><td>Synchronize Model with Errors</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to synchronize the model with the textual notation, even if the notation contains errors. <ac:link ac:anchor="Saving changes in the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td>Name Resolution Error Limit</td><td>10</td><td>Number</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Specify the name resolution error limit to control the handling of name resolution errors. When specified, the name resolution stops if the error limit is reached, leaving the remaining names unresolved. This limit applies independently to each open Textual Editor tab. Set to -1 to disable the error limit and allow name resolution to continue for all names, skipping only those with errors. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Force KerML Namespaces Opening</td><td>false</td><td>true/false</td><td>Set to true to force the opening of KerML namespaces in the Textual Editor.</td></tr><tr><td>Preferred Element Name for Autocompletion</td><td>Short name</td><td><p>Short name</p><p>Name</p><p>Both names</p></td><td>Specify which element name should be suggested in context-sensitive autocompletion. If the selected name type is not specified for an element, autocompletion will suggest an existing name type instead. <ac:link ac:anchor="Autocompletion"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Textual Editor features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Enable Sticky Header</td><td>true</td><td>true/false</td><td><p>Set to true to enable the sticky header in the Textual Editor, which displays the owner of the currently visible notation at the top of the editor. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td>Sticky Header Line Count</td><td>5</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the maximum number of lines to display in the sticky header. Reopen the Textual Editor for the changes to take effect. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Textual Editor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td>Font Size</td><td>14</td><td>Number</td><td>Specify the default font size.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background color : " colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">Text Generation</td></tr><tr><td>Smart Generation Timeout </td><td>-1.0</td><td>-1.0 - ∞</td><td>Specify the timeout duration for smart generation in seconds. During textual notation generation, the tool generates the shortest qualified names. If the timeout is specified, a simpler name is generated once the time limit is reached. Set to -1 to disable the timeout.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=298093924 space=UAF2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: (2026x) Exhibit state

- page_id: `298093924`
- space_key: `UAF2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF2P/pages/298093924/2026x+Exhibit+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:41:58.509+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An exhibit state usage specifies that its owner exhibits a state specified by the exhibit state usage.

An exhibit state usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced state is exhibited by the containing part.
- a state definition or usage, whereby the exhibit state usage represents a "call" from the containing state to the exhibited state.

There are two ways a state can be exhibited by another element:

50%

- by referencing the state usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the exhibit keyword (unnamed element) or the exhibit state keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='exhibit_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='exhibit_state_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']

50%

- by containing an owned exhibit state usage, i.e., the exhibited state is the exhibit state usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the exhibit state keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='exhibit_state_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P'] :
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']
      - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exhibit state' space='UAF2P']

##### Exhibiting a state by referencing a state usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Exhibiting states via the Textual Editor1Exhibiting states via the Textual Editor

To exhibit states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to exhibit a state, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed exhibit state, declare the exhibit keyword followed by the name of the exhibited state.
  2. For a named one, declare the exhibit state keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space='UAF2P'] the exhibited state.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

state vehicleStates; // referenced state

partvehicle{ 
// unnamed exhibit state referencing a state defined elsewhere in the model 
exhibitvehicleStates;

// named exhibit state referencing a state defined elsewhere in the modelexhibitstateoperatingStatereferences vehicleStates;// ORexhibitstateoperatingState::> vehicleStates;

}

###### Exhibiting states via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To exhibit states via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag a state element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to exhibit the state by referencing it using the **Create Exhibit State** command.
  - the exhibit state usage symbol that you want to exhibit the state by referencing it using the **Set Exhibited State** command.

###### Exhibiting statesvia the Specification panel

To exhibit states via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a state element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='UAF2P'] and drop it on the **Exhibited State** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the exhibit state element. The appropriate state is specified for the element.

##### Exhibiting a statevia the ownedexhibited stateusageitselfas theexhibited state

###### Exhibiting states via the Textual Editor

To exhibit states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to exhibit a state, declare the exhibit state keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle{

// exhibited state is the defined exhibit state usage itself contained by the parentexhibitstate vehicleStates;}

###### Exhibiting states via the Create Element command

To exhibit states via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the exhibit state element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the exhibit state's features.

###### Exhibiting states via the view palette

To exhibit states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click the exhibit state button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the exhibit state symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the exhibit state's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// exhibited state is the defined exhibit state usage itself contained by the parent<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">exhibit</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleStates</span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Exhibiting states via the Create Element command</h4><p>To exhibit states via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>exhibit state </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the exhibit state's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Exhibiting states via the view palette</h4><p>To exhibit states via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the States group, click the <strong>exhibit state </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the exhibit state symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the exhibit state's features.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
